**Job description**
The Contracts Engineer – Africa will report to the Country Manager and will be responsible for a wide variety of activities associated with contract management and administration within the region. Implementing and ensuring compliance with company policies and procedures during all phases of the contracting process, pre and post\-award including, category management planning, market sector strategy, strategic sourcing and bid lists, contractor selection and post award supplier management. The growth plan is aggressive with project activity high, you will be expected to interface with the project team/s and apply your expertise to influence the strategy and direction in the areas of Supplier Relationship Management and Contract Management. The position has the potential to create career advancement opportunities.

  

  

**Position Responsibilities**
* Compile contract strategy, draft any amendments to T\&C’s in conjunction with Legal and negotiate contract terms with suppliers


* Establish, maintain and promote robust contract management and claim procedures.


* Liaise with the RSM and engage with the operation to ensure contracts support is available to major CAPEX projects.


* Critical resource in regional tender, evaluation, award and post award process


* Work with the RSM and Supply Manager – Central team to formulate integrated contract plans and other supply chain requirements specific to the region, ensuring compliance with global policies and procedures.


* Support, communicate and implement standards and procedures for tender management, Post Tender Negotiation \& contract award, contract management and standard processes post award.


* Participate in internal and external supplier audits.


* Promote effective communication and good working relationships with Commercial and Legal to ensure reporting is aligned and agreed protocols exist for all activities


* Maintain awareness of purchasing and contract best practice in relation to the oil \& gas market place and act as the functional expert in these matters


* Liaise with senior contracts and procurement specialists for delivery of daily tasks


**The successful candidate should have the following skills and experience:\-**

* Degree qualified in Legal or Business Management.


* Minimum 3 years’ experience in negotiating and managing contracts in the Oil and Gas industry preferably with an Operator, Drilling Contractor or Service Company


* Knowledge and understanding of LOGIC Terms and Conditions


* Experience within a projects environment would be advantageous.


* Strong communication and interpersonal skills and demonstrate solid analytical judgement.

Experience Level: Executive level Experience Length: 10 years
### **Job descriptions \& requirements**

  

Job Summary:  

WTS Energy is seeking a Senior Building Engineer to join their team supporting a major LNG train project in Nigeria. This role is within the oil and gas sector, focusing on industrial building design and construction support for large\-scale energy infrastructure. The Senior Building Engineer will independently manage building design validation, material and vendor engineering, field design changes, and multi\-disciplinary coordination to ensure design consistency and constructability. The position requires leading technical discussions with clients, resolving site issues promptly, and integrating drawings with 3D models to prevent construction conflicts. Candidates can expect a dynamic work environment with opportunities to influence critical project milestones and collaborate across diverse engineering disciplines.  

Responsibilities:  

* Lead the design, review, and validation of building structural systems, layouts, and associated disciplines including waterproofing, roofing, finishing works, and building services integration such as electrical, instrumentation, plumbing, and HVAC.
* Independently manage the full project cycle from design through construction support to close\-out, ensuring compliance with international codes and project specifications.
* Conduct thorough review and approval of Material Approval Requests (MAR), evaluating vendor technical documents and datasheets for compliance and initiating corrective actions when necessary.
* Oversee and manage Field Design Changes (FDC), providing engineering judgment on constructability, proposing alternative design solutions, and ensuring changes are technically sound, cost\-effective, and aligned with project standards.
* Act as the primary technical coordinator for building\-related multi\-disciplinary interfaces, resolving design conflicts and ensuring consistency across electrical, instrumentation, HVAC, piping, and civil works disciplines.
* Lead technical discussions and negotiations with the Client, effectively defending design decisions, addressing comments, and proposing practical alternatives to achieve design closure.
* Review and ensure consistency between Approved for Construction (AFC) drawings, 3D models, and site conditions to identify and resolve mismatches prior to construction.
* Provide prompt and reliable construction support by addressing site queries, construction deviations, and delivering sound engineering decisions under pressure.
* Demonstrate strong ownership and accountability, maintaining clear and firm communication with contractors, subcontractors, the Client, and internal teams to drive project milestones and deliverables.


Qualifications:  

Required Qualifications:  

* Bachelor’s degree in Architectural or Building Engineering
* 10–15 years of proven experience in industrial building design, preferably within Oil \& Gas or LNG projects
* Demonstrated expertise across the full project cycle: Design, Construction Support, and Close\-out phases
* Proficiency in Microsoft Excel for data handling and engineering tracking, Microsoft Word and PowerPoint for technical reporting and presentations, and AutoCAD for drawing review and mark\-up
* Experience with 3D model review software such as PDMS, E3D, or SP3D
* Strong working knowledge of international codes and standards including ASCE, ACI, BS, and Eurocode
* Fluent English communication skills with the ability to convey complex technical information clearly
* Proven ability to independently manage building design and engineering tasks, including structural system validation, waterproofing, roofing, finishing works, and integration of building services (electrical, instrumentation, plumbing, HVAC)
* Expertise in reinforced concrete and steel structural configurations, foundation systems, and multi\-disciplinary interface management
* Skilled in identifying design inconsistencies, interference issues, and associated risks, with the ability to interpret and apply project specifications and international codes to practical design and site conditions
* Experience in material and vendor engineering, including review and approval of Material Approval Requests (MAR), evaluation of vendor technical documents, and ensuring compliance with project specifications
* Competence in managing field design changes, providing engineering judgment on constructability, and delivering technically sound, cost\-effective design solutions
* Strong multi\-disciplinary coordination skills to manage complex interface issues and ensure design consistency across electrical, instrumentation, HVAC, piping, and civil works
* Ability to lead technical discussions with clients, defend design decisions, negotiate effectively, and propose practical alternatives
* Capability to review and ensure consistency between Approved for Construction (AFC) drawings, 3D models, and site conditions
* Experience providing construction support and resolving site queries and deviations under pressure


Preferred Qualifications:  

* Experience working on brownfield projects with site constraints
* Deep understanding of constructability principles and temporary versus permanent solution strategies
* Familiarity with code\-based design justification and deviation handling processes (Technical Queries, Deviations, Letters)
* Knowledge of lifting, installation constraints, and equipment interface considerations
* Demonstrated ability to work independently with minimal supervision, exhibiting strong ownership and accountability
* Effective communication skills for engaging with contractors, subcontractors, clients, and internal teams under high\-pressure situations


What We Offer:  

* Comprehensive health maintenance organization (HMO) coverage provided by WTS Energy to support employee well\-being
* Employer and employee pension contributions ensuring long\-term financial security
* Accommodation and feeding provided during on\-site assignments at Bonny NLNG Train 7 project
* Logistics support arranged from designated pickup points to project site to facilitate smooth commuting
* Opportunity to work on a high\-profile industrial project within the Oil \& Gas / LNG sector, enhancing professional experience
* Exposure to multidisciplinary coordination and leadership roles fostering career growth and technical development
* Dynamic work environment with structured rotation cycles promoting work\-life balance during off periods
* Engagement with international codes and standards, advancing technical expertise and industry best practices

### **Job descriptions \& requirements**

  

**Job Title: Project Manager, Electrical \& Instrumentation (Oil \& Gas)**


**Salary(Net):** 700,000\-850,000 (NGN) per month


**Location:** Victoria Island


**Location:** Lagos, Nigeria


**Employment Type:** Full Time


Minimum of 13 years’ experience  



  

How to Apply: interested and qualified candidates should send CVs to elevarehumansolutions@gmail.com using the role title as the mail subject.


**Role Overview**


**We are seeking a seasoned Project Manager, Electrical and Instrumentation with strong oil and gas experience to drive the execution of complex E\&I projects across upstream, midstream, or downstream operations.**


**The role requires deep technical expertise in electrical systems, instrumentation, and control within hazardous and high compliance environments, combined with strong project delivery capability. The ideal candidate will manage full project lifecycle delivery while ensuring alignment with industry standards, safety regulations, and commercial targets.**

  


**Key Responsibilities**


* **Lead end to end execution of Electrical and Instrumentation projects within oil and gas facilities**


* **Develop and manage project schedules, budgets, scope, and resource plans in line with EPC standards**


* **Oversee design, installation, testing, and commissioning of E\&I systems including PLC, SCADA, and DCS**


* **Ensure compliance with international oil and gas standards such as IEC, NEC, ATEX, and ISO requirements**


* **Coordinate multidisciplinary teams across engineering, procurement, construction, and commissioning phases**


* **Manage contractors, subcontractors, and OEM vendors to ensure performance delivery and cost efficiency**


* **Drive HSE compliance, ensuring zero harm culture across all project sites**


* **Identify and manage project risks, including technical, operational, and commercial exposures**


* **Provide detailed project reporting and performance insights to senior leadership and clients**


* **Support tendering and business development processes with technical and commercial input**

  


**Requirements**


* **Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Instrumentation Engineering, or related discipline**


* **Minimum of 13 years’ experience in Electrical and Instrumentation within the oil and gas sector**


* **Proven experience managing E\&I projects in upstream, midstream, or downstream operations**


* **Strong knowledge of hazardous area classification and explosion proof systems**


* **Hands on experience with control systems such as PLC, SCADA, and DCS**


* **Experience working within EPC project environments**


* **PMP or equivalent project management certification is strongly preferred**

description:
* We are seeking a competent and detail\-oriented Quantity Surveyor / Quality Assurance Officer to oversee cost management, contract administration, and quality control for water infrastructure projects.
* The ideal candidate must have hands\-on experience in cost estimation, BOQ preparation, contract administration, and implementation of quality assurance procedures on projects such as water treatment plants, reservoirs, transmission mains, pumping stations, and distribution networks.
* Experience handling donor\-funded, NGO\-sponsored, or government infrastructure projects is highly desirable.
* The role requires strong financial oversight capability combined with the ability to enforce quality standards and ensure compliance with project specifications and regulatory requirements.


**Key Responsibilities**  

Cost Management \& Quantity Surveying:


* Prepare cost estimates, budgets, and Bills of Quantities (BOQs) for water infrastructure projects.
* Conduct tender analysis and support procurement processes.
* Prepare interim valuations, payment certificates, and final accounts.
* Assess variations, claims, and change orders.
* Monitor project costs and ensure adherence to approved budgets.


Quality Assurance \& Compliance:


* Develop and implement Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) plans.
* Ensure construction activities comply with approved drawings, specifications, and standards.
* Conduct site inspections to verify quality of materials and workmanship.
* Monitor testing of materials (concrete, pipes, fittings, etc.) and verify compliance.
* Ensure corrective actions are implemented where deficiencies are identified.


Contract \& Donor Compliance:


* Ensure adherence to Nigerian procurement regulations and donor guidelines.
* Maintain proper documentation for audit and reporting purposes.
* Support project audits and compliance reviews.
* Assist in ensuring environmental and safety standards are maintained.


Project Coordination:


* Work closely with Project Managers, Engineers, and contractors.
* Attend site meetings and provide commercial and quality advisory support.
* Verify executed quantities and certify works completed.


**Requirements**  

Education \& Experience:


* BSc/HND in Quantity Surveying, Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or related field.
* Minimum of 7 years of relevant experience in quantity surveying and/or quality assurance for infrastructure projects.
* Proven experience in water infrastructure, civil works, or public works projects is required.
* Experience handling donor\-funded, NGO\-sponsored, or government projects is highly desirable.


Professional Certification (Compulsory):


* Registered with QSRBN (Quantity Surveyors Registration Board of Nigeria) – Mandatory (for QS background).
* Membership of NIQS (Nigerian Institute of Quantity Surveyors) – Mandatory (if QS).

Technical Competencies:


* Strong knowledge of BOQ preparation, cost control, and contract administration.
* Familiarity with FIDIC or similar standard contract forms.
* Strong understanding of QA/QC procedures in civil and water infrastructure works.
* Ability to interpret technical drawings and specifications.
* Proficiency in MS Excel and cost management tools.


Personal Attributes:


* High attention to detail and strong analytical skills.
* Strong integrity and compliance\-driven mindset.
* Excellent negotiation and reporting skills.
* Ability to manage financial and quality responsibilities simultaneously.
* Willingness to travel for site inspections and project monitoring.


Other Requirements:


* Willingness to relocate to project locations in Niger State or Plateau State as required.


**Salary**  

N350,000 \- N500,000 / Month.

**Operations Geologist**
========================

**TAG E \& P LIMITED**
----------------------

**Research, Teaching \& Training**
----------------------------------

 New

### **Share link**

### **Job descriptions \& requirements**

  

**Company Description**


TAG E \& P LIMITED is a leading business supplies and equipment company headquartered in Castlewood House, New Oxford Street, London, United Kingdom. The company is known for its commitment to delivering high\-quality services and innovative solutions. TAG E \& P LIMITED operates in the energy sector, providing specialized services to support efficient operations. Our team is dedicated to excellence, innovation, and fostering growth in the industries we serve.

  


**Role Description**


This Operations Geologist role is responsible for the planning and execution of Client’s drill\-wells in Nigeria. To accomplish this the Operations Geologist must be competent in well planning and surveillance, integrated pressure prediction, and site investigation; understanding key deliverables


such as a top\-hole prognosis, pore\-pressure prediction, and completed well\-design. Key to the successful drilling of a well is close communication with the Business Unit (Geoscience, Reservoir Engineers), Petrophysicist, and Wells Engineers throughout the design and execution phases of the


project, hence the Operations Geologist must be a good communicator, integrator and be highly organized to effectively change tact as conditions dictate. Evaluating multiple scenarios and ensuring close communication between all parties involved whilst managing expectations to ensure a safe, timely and economic completion of the drilling program meeting all geologic and operational


objectives.

  


**Responsibilities**


* Demonstrate safety leadership and uphold Client’s safety culture ensuring No Spills and the Nobody Gets Hurt, both in the office and if required, on the rig site.
* Strong understanding of integrated pore pressure prediction and surveillance
* Strong understanding of site investigation including shallow drilling hazards evaluation (top\-hole prognosis) and riserless drilling geohazards
* Well planning and surveillance (may include but not limited to):
* Coordinate with the Wells organization and the project Petrophysicist in the contract technical evaluation for MWD/LWD, Wireline, and Mud Logging services (Procurement Tendering)
* Evaluate technical services and compile cost estimates for Wellsite Geology, Wellsite Biostratigraphy, Core Handling/Preservation and Analysis, and wellsite data managemen services as needed
* Compile and document Well Objectives, Formation Evaluation plan, operations \& communication protocols, and data/sample distribution into a Geological Program
* Compile well and evaluation plan information in support of Company regulatory groups (Regulatory)
* Participate/lead regular coordination and planning meetings with the Wells and Geoscience Teams
* Facilitate and communicate with Wells Engineers and project geologists to produce a well plan and casing design which will enable complete evaluation of geologic objectives whilst maintaining an economic and cost\-effective solution
* Design and establishment of casing seat and TD selection protocols, coring and formation evaluation programs.
* Supervision, logistical and technical support of Wellsite Geologic personnel (Wellsite Geologists, Service Company personnel) to gather complete, high quality geologic data sets as specified.
* QC and local loading, storage, and archival of all relevant electronic and physical data using systematic file naming and organization.
* Capture lessons learned for future operational improvements
* Assist, if necessary, in wellsite\-based activities, including selection of casing seats, Geosteering (using Azimuthal Ultra Deep Resistivity), wireline logging and formation testing programs.
* Review and analysis of geologic evaluation programs and service company performance on a well by well or monthly/quarterly basis to identify and address areas with room for improvement.
* Compile and distribute final well reports, logs, and supporting documents to all parties in a timely fashion.
* Presenting technical work to Subsurface and Wells teams including but not limited to TD protocol reviews, prespud meetings and after\-action reviews (AAR).

  


  



**Skills and Qualifications**


* BS/MS in Geology from a recognized institution
* Experience as an Operations Geologist
* Offshore (barge, jackup, MODU) drilling experience
* Fluent in both written and spoken English, have good organizational and coordination skills, and be able to work well with others.
* Flexible to work outside 8am\-5pm to provide 24 hours Ops Geo coverage during drilling and logging operations.
* Flexible to work on Saturday, Sunday, and public holiday to provide 24 hours Ops Geo coverage during drilling and logging operations.
* Offshore experience including platform/deviated well drilling with associated technologies (e.g., LWD, WL).
* Must be in good physical condition and shall comply with all project health and safety requirements (including any rig or country\-specific certifications required to work offshore).
* May be required: Have successfully passed a recognized (OPITO) course in Helicopter Underwater Escape Training (HUET), Sea Survival, and H2S within the previous two years. Certifications must be current and not due to expire for the anticipated duration of the project.
* Experience with mud logging; cuttings description; real\-time image logs; and correlating them to determine lithology and well path.
* Experience with basic FE interpretation for identifying rock type, fluid type, borehole conditions, etc.
* Experience utilizing azimuthal ultra\-deep resistivity (AUDR) for geosteering deepwater highly directional/deviated wells
* Experience with managed pressure drilling (MPD) practices
* Experience with production drilling environments and challenges, i.e. depleted sand, swept compartments, wellbore stability, fluid type uncertainty, etc.
* Experience with directional drilling, well planning, depth control measurements; and understanding of BHA's (Bottom Hole Assembly).
* Experience with coring operations.
* Experience of working in integrated asset team environment.
* Experience in applying structural geology concepts and methods to assess compartmentalization/communication.
* Experience of having worked with geologic modelers to incorporate maps and their interpretations into 3D geologic models.

  


Additional Skills


(Would be of advantage).


* 2D, 3D \& Volume Interpretation and Mapping, AVO Analysis \& Time/Depth Conversion, SeismicStratigraphy, High Resolution Seismic Program Design and Oversight, Engineering Geophysics Data Analysis and Integration, Geotechnical Data Integration, Shallow Top Hole Well Planning, Vendor Contracting, Supervision and evaluation.
* Geologic Scenario Development, Physical Properties Analysis, Burial History Modeling, Velocity Based Pressure Prediction Evaluation, Pressure Prediction Integration for Well Planning, While Drilling Evaluation of LWD logs and drilling parameters.
* Well Planning and Casing Design, OIMS and Regulatory compliance, Casing Seat Selection Protocols, Coring and FE Program Design, FE Vendor Contracting and Evaluation, Well Site Supervision, Well Surveillance \& Program Execution, Post\-Drill follow\-up and integrationv.

QED International Oil and Gas Nigeria Limited is a company with a strong presence in the energy industry, specializing in oil and gas services. Based in Nigeria, the company is dedicated to delivering innovative and efficient solutions to meet the dynamic needs of the oil and gas sector. QED International prides itself on its commitment to operational excellence, safety, and the development of sustainable energy solutions. The company fosters a collaborative and progressive work environment, valuing expertise and a proactive approach to industry challenges.

  


**Location: Lagos and Bonny**

  


**Key Responsibilities:**


**1\. Business Development \& Client Management**


* Identify and develop new business opportunities within the manpower outsourcing, recruitment, and staffing sectors.


* Conduct strategic market mapping and develop growth strategies targeting specific industries (oil and gas, engineering, construction, etc.).


* Develop and execute sales strategies that align with company goals, revenue targets and expansion plans.


* Maintain a strong pipeline of prospective clients through research, networking, cold calls, industry events and referrals.


* Collaborate with senior management to set business targets and design manpower development programs aligned with market demands.


* Evaluate and recommend partnerships, joint ventures or collaborations that enhance the manpower service offerings.


**2\. Client Relationship Management**


* Serve as the primary liaison between clients and internal teams for all manpower\-related services.


* Build, maintain and strengthen long\-term relationships with existing and prospective clients.


* Schedule and lead client meetings to understand staffing requirements, project timelines and performance expectations.


* Manage client feedback, address service concerns and ensure continuous improvement in delivery standards.


* Facilitate client satisfaction surveys and implement corrective actions where necessary.


**3\. Project \& Operational Coordination**


* Work closely with the Recruitment, HR and Operations teams to ensure timely sourcing, onboarding and deployment of qualified personnel.


* Assist manpower mobilization, demobilization and replacement processes according to project schedules.


* Ensure that all deployed staff meet client specifications in terms of skill, certification and conduct.


* Oversee timesheet management, attendance reporting and contract renewals for outsourced staff.


* Track workforce performance metrics, discipline issues and turnover rates; provide reports and recommendations for improvement.


* Coordinate all pre\-deployment documentation, including background checks, compliance forms and client acceptance.


**4\. Market Research \& Strategy**


* Conduct market intelligence to identify trends, competitors and potential clients in manpower services.


* Support in developing strategies to expand market share and improve brand visibility.


* Provide feedback and reports on market activities and client needs to inform business strategy.


**5\. Proposal \& Documentation Management**


* Prepare technical and commercial proposals, expressions of interest (EOIs), capability statements and tender submissions for manpower projects.


* Collaborate with the proposal team to develop tailored solutions that meet client requirements and budgetary expectations.


* Ensure proper documentation of all business development activities, leads, contracts etc.


* Maintain an updated database of manpower projects, client records and performance reports.

  


**Qualifications**


* Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Human Resource Management, Marketing, or related field.


* Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in business development, preferably in manpower supply, recruitment, or outsourcing.


* Proven track record in client acquisition and relationship management.


* Ability to travel as required.

  


Qualified candidates can send their cv to recruitment@qedi\-ng.com or hr@qedi\-ng.com

Job Title: Sales Director, Oil \& Gas Market (Nigeria)Job Responsibilities

  

Fully responsible for business development and in\-depth market cultivation in Nigeria’s oil and gas sector. Leveraging the group’s factory production capacity and global supply chain advantages, deeply explore and maintain local customer resources; coordinate market development, customer expansion, business negotiation, order signing and full\-process project follow\-up, build and lead the local sales team, and efficiently achieve annual sales performance targets.


Job Requirements


* Familiar with major oil operators, large/medium/small oilfield service companies and local oil \& gas equipment procurement traders in Nigeria, with complete upstream industry customer channels.
* Possess years of in\-depth working experience in Nigeria’s local oil and gas industry, with mature and high\-quality senior industry connections.
* Deeply understand the local market business ecosystem, oilfield project bidding processes and full equipment procurement procedures, with rich experience in local market operations.
* Have high\-quality industry cooperation foundations and connections with Nigerian oil operators, EPC contractors and other relevant institutions.
* Possess rich experience in overseas sales of oil and gas equipment, familiar with equipment working conditions, on\-site construction requirements and full project implementation processes.
* Fluent in English, with excellent business negotiation, customer development and business docking capabilities, able to conduct smooth cross\-cultural communication.
* Strong awareness of market development, with the ability to independently operate regional markets and solve business problems.
* Possess professional team management capabilities, able to independently build and lead the local overseas sales team to improve team performance.
* Both local Nigerian candidates and Chinese nationals permanently based in Nigeria are welcome to apply.


We Offer


Base salary \+ high sales commission with no upper limit for incentives; backed by the strong supply chain and in\-house factory resources of Ruiyuan Holdings Group, we provide broad career development and promotion opportunities for individuals to help them grow together with the company.

===============================================

**Carogas Energy Limited**
--------------------------

**Management \& Business Development**
--------------------------------------

Today
 New
 Rest of Nigeria (Plateau) Full Time Mining, Energy \& Metals Confidential
### **Share link**

Experience Level: Senior level Experience Length: 5 years
### **Job descriptions \& requirements**

  

**About Carogas**


Carogas is building Nigeria's first structured, quality\-controlled barite supply chain for the upstream oil and gas. We are hiring a Field Supervisor to anchor our on\-ground operations across our gathering hub in Plateau State.

  


**The Role**


You will be our primary representative on the ground, receiving barite ore from artisanal miners, running XRF quality tests, managing stockpiles, coordinating truck loading and dispatch, and enforcing Carogas's intake and payment standards. This is a hands\-on field role. You will spend most of your time at hub sites and mine locations.

  


**What You Will Be Doing**


* Receiving and registering barite deliveries from artisanal miners and mine owners
* Operating portable XRF devices to test and grade incoming ore
* Managing stockpile segregation and inventory records at each hub
* Sorting accepted ore by quality tier, overseeing washing where required, and supervising bagging and weighing of material into bags
* Supervising loading of bags onto haulage trucks and ensuring documentation is complete
* Building relationships with miners across the zone
* Enforcing payment controls, conducting daily reconciliations, and submitting field reports
* Maintaining hub security, access control, and HSE standards

  


**What We Are Looking For**


* Degree in Mining Engineering, Geology, Mineral Processing, or a related field
* 5–7 years of field experience in solid minerals or mining operations in Nigeria
* Ability to speak Hausa is a strong advantage for effective communication with miners and local communities across the hub zones
* Real, hands\-on experience working with artisanal and small\-scale miners. You must understand how they operate and how to earn their trust
* Practical knowledge of barite or other industrial minerals
* Strong logistics instincts, comfortable coordinating loading, transport, and documentation
* Integrity, operational discipline, and the ability to work independently in the field
* XRF experience is a plus; existing miner relationships in Plateau is a strong advantage

  


**Why Join Carogas**


This is a ground\-floor opportunity to play a central role in building something new in Nigerian solid minerals. You will have real responsibility, direct field ownership, and room to grow as the operation scales toward full beneficiation and export.

  


Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.

**Operations Geologist**
========================

**TAG E \& P LIMITED**
----------------------

**Research, Teaching \& Training**
----------------------------------

Today
 New
 Lagos Full Time Retail, Fashion \& FMCG Confidential
### **Share link**

### **Job descriptions \& requirements**

  

**Company Description**


TAG E \& P LIMITED is a leading business supplies and equipment company headquartered in Castlewood House, New Oxford Street, London, United Kingdom. The company is known for its commitment to delivering high\-quality services and innovative solutions. TAG E \& P LIMITED operates in the energy sector, providing specialized services to support efficient operations. Our team is dedicated to excellence, innovation, and fostering growth in the industries we serve.

  


**Role Description**


This Operations Geologist role is responsible for the planning and execution of Client’s drill\-wells in Nigeria. To accomplish this the Operations Geologist must be competent in well planning and surveillance, integrated pressure prediction, and site investigation; understanding key deliverables


such as a top\-hole prognosis, pore\-pressure prediction, and completed well\-design. Key to the successful drilling of a well is close communication with the Business Unit (Geoscience, Reservoir Engineers), Petrophysicist, and Wells Engineers throughout the design and execution phases of the


project, hence the Operations Geologist must be a good communicator, integrator and be highly organized to effectively change tact as conditions dictate. Evaluating multiple scenarios and ensuring close communication between all parties involved whilst managing expectations to ensure a safe, timely and economic completion of the drilling program meeting all geologic and operational


objectives.

  


**Responsibilities**


* Demonstrate safety leadership and uphold Client’s safety culture ensuring No Spills and the Nobody Gets Hurt, both in the office and if required, on the rig site.
* Strong understanding of integrated pore pressure prediction and surveillance
* Strong understanding of site investigation including shallow drilling hazards evaluation (top\-hole prognosis) and riserless drilling geohazards
* Well planning and surveillance (may include but not limited to):
* Coordinate with the Wells organization and the project Petrophysicist in the contract technical evaluation for MWD/LWD, Wireline, and Mud Logging services (Procurement Tendering)
* Evaluate technical services and compile cost estimates for Wellsite Geology, Wellsite Biostratigraphy, Core Handling/Preservation and Analysis, and wellsite data managemen services as needed
* Compile and document Well Objectives, Formation Evaluation plan, operations \& communication protocols, and data/sample distribution into a Geological Program
* Compile well and evaluation plan information in support of Company regulatory groups (Regulatory)
* Participate/lead regular coordination and planning meetings with the Wells and Geoscience Teams
* Facilitate and communicate with Wells Engineers and project geologists to produce a well plan and casing design which will enable complete evaluation of geologic objectives whilst maintaining an economic and cost\-effective solution
* Design and establishment of casing seat and TD selection protocols, coring and formation evaluation programs.
* Supervision, logistical and technical support of Wellsite Geologic personnel (Wellsite Geologists, Service Company personnel) to gather complete, high quality geologic data sets as specified.
* QC and local loading, storage, and archival of all relevant electronic and physical data using systematic file naming and organization.
* Capture lessons learned for future operational improvements
* Assist, if necessary, in wellsite\-based activities, including selection of casing seats, Geosteering (using Azimuthal Ultra Deep Resistivity), wireline logging and formation testing programs.
* Review and analysis of geologic evaluation programs and service company performance on a well by well or monthly/quarterly basis to identify and address areas with room for improvement.
* Compile and distribute final well reports, logs, and supporting documents to all parties in a timely fashion.
* Presenting technical work to Subsurface and Wells teams including but not limited to TD protocol reviews, prespud meetings and after\-action reviews (AAR).

  


  



**Skills and Qualifications**


* BS/MS in Geology from a recognized institution
* Experience as an Operations Geologist
* Offshore (barge, jackup, MODU) drilling experience
* Fluent in both written and spoken English, have good organizational and coordination skills, and be able to work well with others.
* Flexible to work outside 8am\-5pm to provide 24 hours Ops Geo coverage during drilling and logging operations.
* Flexible to work on Saturday, Sunday, and public holiday to provide 24 hours Ops Geo coverage during drilling and logging operations.
* Offshore experience including platform/deviated well drilling with associated technologies (e.g., LWD, WL).
* Must be in good physical condition and shall comply with all project health and safety requirements (including any rig or country\-specific certifications required to work offshore).
* May be required: Have successfully passed a recognized (OPITO) course in Helicopter Underwater Escape Training (HUET), Sea Survival, and H2S within the previous two years. Certifications must be current and not due to expire for the anticipated duration of the project.
* Experience with mud logging; cuttings description; real\-time image logs; and correlating them to determine lithology and well path.
* Experience with basic FE interpretation for identifying rock type, fluid type, borehole conditions, etc.
* Experience utilizing azimuthal ultra\-deep resistivity (AUDR) for geosteering deepwater highly directional/deviated wells
* Experience with managed pressure drilling (MPD) practices
* Experience with production drilling environments and challenges, i.e. depleted sand, swept compartments, wellbore stability, fluid type uncertainty, etc.
* Experience with directional drilling, well planning, depth control measurements; and understanding of BHA's (Bottom Hole Assembly).
* Experience with coring operations.
* Experience of working in integrated asset team environment.
* Experience in applying structural geology concepts and methods to assess compartmentalization/communication.
* Experience of having worked with geologic modelers to incorporate maps and their interpretations into 3D geologic models.

  


Additional Skills


(Would be of advantage).


* 2D, 3D \& Volume Interpretation and Mapping, AVO Analysis \& Time/Depth Conversion, SeismicStratigraphy, High Resolution Seismic Program Design and Oversight, Engineering Geophysics Data Analysis and Integration, Geotechnical Data Integration, Shallow Top Hole Well Planning, Vendor Contracting, Supervision and evaluation.
* Geologic Scenario Development, Physical Properties Analysis, Burial History Modeling, Velocity Based Pressure Prediction Evaluation, Pressure Prediction Integration for Well Planning, While Drilling Evaluation of LWD logs and drilling parameters.
* Well Planning and Casing Design, OIMS and Regulatory compliance, Casing Seat Selection Protocols, Coring and FE Program Design, FE Vendor Contracting and Evaluation, Well Site Supervision, Well Surveillance \& Program Execution, Post\-Drill follow\-up and integrationv.

?LifeBank is a technology company that powers hospitals and care centers to deliver quality healthcare to patients in emerging markets. The company’s technology, infrastructure, agile production, and distribution networks enable hospitals in emerging markets to catch up with their global peers. The company has distributed 45,000\+ medical products to 1000\+ healthcare facilities, saving 18,000\+ lives across Nigeria, Ethiopia and Kenya. At LifeBank, we are the technology\-led healthcare system for the future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:


**Job Position: Customer Acquisition Agent**


**Job Location:** Onipanu, Lagos  

Employment Type: Full\-time  

Department: Growth / Sales / Customer Acquisition  

Reports to: Director, Customer Health \&Acquisition


**Role Summary**


LifeBank is a technology company that powers hospitals and care centers to deliver quality healthcare to patients in emerging markets. The company’s technology, infrastructure, agile production, and distribution networks enable hospitals in emerging markets to catch up with their global peers. The company has distributed 45,000\+ medical products to 1000\+ healthcare facilities, saving 18,000\+ lives across Nigeria, Ethiopia and Kenya. At LifeBank, we are the technology\-led healthcare system for the future.


We are recruiting to fill the position below:


**Job Position: Customer Acquisition Agent**


**Job Location:** Onipanu, Lagos  

Employment Type: Full\-time  

Department: Growth / Sales / Customer Acquisition  

Reports to: Director, Customer Health \&Acquisition


**Role Summary**


* The Customer Acquisition Agent is responsible for identifying, engaging, and converting new healthcare facilities, hospitals, and clinics into active customers for LifeBank.
* The role focuses on expanding the company’s customer base, driving revenue growth, and ensuring seamless onboarding of new accounts into LifeBank’s healthcare logistics ecosystem.
* You will be the frontline growth driver, building trust with healthcare providers and positioning LifeBank as the preferred partner for medical supply and logistics solutions.


**Key Responsibilities**  

Customer Acquisition \& Sales:


* Identify and prospect new hospitals, clinics, pharmacies, and healthcare facilities.
* Conduct cold calls, visits, and outreach to generate new business opportunities.
* Present LifeBank’s services and value proposition to potential customers.
* Convert qualified leads into active paying customers.
* Achieve monthly and quarterly acquisition targets.


Field Engagement \& Relationship Building:


* Conduct on\-site visits to healthcare facilities to understand their needs.
* Build strong relationships with decision\-makers (doctors, procurement officers, administrators).
* Maintain consistent follow\-up with prospects until conversion.
* Represent LifeBank professionally in all field interactions.


Onboarding \& Activation:


* Support new customers through onboarding and first\-order activation.
* Ensure smooth handover to customer service and operations teams.
* Educate customers on ordering processes, platforms, and service standards.


Market Intelligence:


* Gather insights on competitor activity, pricing, and service gaps.
* Identify underserved areas and new market opportunities.
* Provide feedback to the growth and product teams on customer needs.


Reporting \& CRM Management:


* Maintain accurate records of leads, visits, and conversions in CRM tools.
* Submit daily/weekly reports on sales activities and pipeline progress.
* Track performance against acquisition targets.


**Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)**


* Number of new customers acquired monthly
* Conversion rate from leads to active accounts
* Revenue generated from new accounts
* Number of successful field visits and engagements
* Customer onboarding success rate
* Pipeline size and growth


**Key Requirements**  

Education \& Experience:


* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Business, Marketing, Health Sciences, or related field.
* 2–4 years of experience in sales, customer acquisition, field marketing, or business development (healthcare or logistics is an advantage).


Skills \& Competencies:


* Strong persuasion, negotiation, and communication skills.
* Comfortable with field work and frequent customer visits.
* Self\-motivated and target\-driven.
* Ability to build trust quickly with healthcare professionals.
* Basic CRM and digital tool proficiency.
* Strong resilience and ability to handle rejection.


What Success Looks Like:


* Consistent growth in new healthcare facilities signed on.
* Strong pipeline of qualified prospects across assigned regions.
* Smooth onboarding experience for new customers.
* Increased revenue contribution from new acquisitions.
* Strong presence of LifeBank across targeted healthcare networks.


**Salary**  

N120,000 \- N150,000 / Month.

**Boskalis**

**Engineering \& Technology**

### **Share link**

### **Job descriptions \& requirements**

  

How you can make your mark  

Make your mark as Technical Superintendent Nigeria at Smit Lamnalco  

You will manage and execute running ‘day\-to\-day’ Maintenance and Repair (M\&R) activities of vessels assigned to you within time\-frame and budget as set, in order to maintain technical reliability and maximize utilization of the fleet.  

**Your Responsibilities**

  

* Coordinate and execute ‘day\-to\-day’ running maintenance activities (mainly preventive) onboard the vessels to maintain technical reliability of vessels and prevent downtime/off\-hire.
* Supervise, coach and instruct chief engineers onboard the vessels to ensure technical management onboard is in line with company expectation
* Execute repairs, supervise crew and subcontractors in line with the agreed planning, budget and quality standards.
* Take responsibility for the correct allocation of costs related to the vessel’s repair and maintenance, dockings, damages and modifications.
* Guides crew on board the vessels to ensure Company standard are met and work is done as per guidelines and specifications
* Actively promotes Safety Health Environmental and Quality (SHEQ) awareness and monitors performance to ensure safety standards and safe working practices /leads ‘by example’
* IInterface with Regulatory Authorities \& Stakeholders i.e. Classification Society \& Flag State on technical integrity of vessels whilst maintaining cordial relationship with stakeholders.

  

To fulfil the position of Technical Superintendent Nigiria you are in the possession of:

  

* Experience as technical superintendent or technical manager in a similar environment / company.
* At least higher / secondary vocational education in mechanical engineering, naval architect or equivalent.
* Experience as former chief engineer on offshore support Vessels is preferred.
* At least 7 years verifiable experience gained as technical superintendent/port engineer or technical manager.
* Fluent command of the English language, spoken and written.

  

We offer  

What you can expect

  

* A dynamic environment: A job that allows you to collaborate with a talented team of experts from different backgrounds and contribute to making a significant impact.
* Rewarding conditions: This is a great opportunity for a talented and motivated technical professional who is keen and eager to join an International Marine company. Smit Lamnalco offers a competitive compensation \& benefit package.
* Career development: We offer you plenty of opportunities to bring out the best in yourself .

  

Extra information

  

* Your team: You will be part of the Fleet Management Department of AfrikDelta Marine Ltd, operational unit fully controlled by Smit Lamnalco which consist of approximately 10 professionals. You will report to the Regional Fleet Manager.
* Where you will work: You will work in Onne (Port Harcourt) on a rotation of 8/8 weeks and may be required to travel occasionally.
* Full/part\-time job: The position of technical superintendent is a full\-time job, work hours to be adjusted as per operational requirement.
* Next steps: Apply easily by completing the online application form. Interviews are held online or in the office. Once it’s clear we’re a good match, we’ll make you an offer – and look forward to welcoming you to the company.

  

**About Boskalis**  

Smit Lamnalco is part of the Boskalis group of companies. Working at Smit Lamnalco is about providing marine support services tailored to the unique operational needs of our global customers in local markets. Our promise to our clients is to provide the best marine services possible, no matter how challenging the environment or how remote the location.  

In Nigeria, Smit Lamnalco provides technical services to AfrikDelta Marine Limited (ADML). ADML is a 100% Nigerian owned marine and offshore services company, established to provide premium, safe, reliable and efficient services to the major oil and gas companies in Nigeria and the Gulf of Guinea.  

ADML was established in July 2012 providing quality service to the Nigeria’s offshore oil and gas production. ADML is a major player in the Nigeria Oil and Gas industry with focus on Midstream and Offshore Terminal Marine operations. The company is managed by an experienced team of marine industry expert and has become the leading indigenous provider of marine services in the region.  

Interested?  

We’ll be happy to answer your questions about the position of technical superintendent Nigeria. Please contact Sandra Hofstede, senior corporate recruiter via 06\-46042957\.  

Please apply via our website before 20th April.  

Disclaimer for Recruiters and Recruitment Agencies  

We appreciate your interest in our vacancies and understand that your candidate might be enthusiastic about this exciting opportunity. However, our recruitment process is not structured this way, at Boskalis we handle recruitment ourselves. Therefore we do not accept unsolicited applications or CVs from recruitment agencies. Any submission will be treated as a direct application.  

Please note: Boskalis never requests a financial contribution to arrange visas or other documents related to a job vacancy. Be wary of individuals who pretend to be our recruiters and do ask for such contributions.

Job summary
We are currently seeking a Senior Reservoir Engineer who will be responsible for managing and optimizing reservoir performance to maximize oil and gas recovery.
Min Qualification: Degree Experience Level: Senior level Experience Length: 5 years Language Requirement: English Working Hours: Full Time - 9 to 5 Applicant Location: Port Harcourt & Rivers State, Nigeria
Job descriptions & requirements
Responsibilities:

Conducting reservoir studies
Developing production forecasts
Supporting field development planning.

Requirements:

Applicants must have a Bachelor’s degree in Petroleum Engineering or a related Engineering field.
Applicants must have a minimum of 5 years of relevant experience in reservoir engineering.
Must have strong experience in reservoir studies, reserves estimation, and production forecasting.
Must have experience with reservoir simulation and material balance analysis.
Applicants must be proficient in reservoir engineering software such as Petrel, Eclipse, MBAL, PROSPER, or GAP.
Must have experience in field development planning and reservoir management.
Applicants must have strong analytical, problem-solving, and technical reporting skills.

Remuneration: NGN 700,000-800,000

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES




	Oversee Modular Refinery operations
	Manage Tank Farm & Depot activities
	Supervise Marine Logistics & Haulage
	Coordinate Supply Chain operations
	Lead Integrated Waste Management TDU
	Oversee Industrial & Medical Oxygen Production


KEY REQUIREMENTS




	Engineering Degree Mechanical, Production, Chemical
	COREN Certification Mandatory
	15+ years in Oil & Gas / Industrial Operations
	Strong multi-site leadership experience
	Proven ability to drive efficiency & compliance

job_title: Engineering (Chemical, Petrochemical, Mechanical, Civil, Production) Graduate Programme
description:
What’s the role 



Join us as a Graduate at Shell in Nigeria where you will embark on a comprehensive 3-year program designed to develop future leaders.



The Graduate Recruitment within Shell follows a standard global methodology used to attract, select, and place our best talent within the various Shell businesses. For 2026, we are recruiting post-NYSC graduates



What you’ll be doing 



You will showcase your capacity to analyze situations and propose purposeful solutions that align with the company’s strategic goals - adaptability to changing environments, learner mindset, drive to deliver results - and collaborate and nurture relationships as you work effectively with a diverse team. You will work with experienced colleagues and will share your unique perspective and fresh ideas on how to tackle energy-related challenges. Our inclusive and collaborative culture will provide you with the support you need to grow your skills as you work on meaningful projects with exciting responsibilities right from the start. 



What you bring 



We are looking for a candidate who thrives on new challenges and embraces innovation. You should be confident in your ability to absorb information, analyze problems, make objective decisions, and generate original ideas. The ideal candidate will possess the drive and resilience to achieve goals, the flexibility to collaborate effectively within a team, and the credibility to influence others. Being open to alternative perspectives and views is essential, using them to refine ideas and outcomes. 



Eligibility 



To be eligible for the Shell Graduate Program: 




	You must have graduated Bachelor’s or Master’s before your start date
	You must have less than three years of work experience.
	You must have completed the National Youth Service Corps NYSC programme
	You must possess strong communication and interpersonal skills.


Please indicate your NYSC Certificate or NYSC Exemption in your CV. 



Submitting more than one application will lead to automatic disqualification of all your applications.

MAIN FUNCTIONS




	Responsible for planning, coordinating and controlling the day-to-day field operations for the assigned crafts and subcontractors. A typical scope of responsibility would be Field Superintendent or Area Superintendent.


TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIES



In addition to all Level 1 and 2 tasks, a Level 3 Construction Superintendent will:




	Assist in preparation and review for submittals, RFIs and RFCs and make recommendations regarding suitability
	Review contractor health and safety plans, traffic control and erosion control measures on projects
	Verify quantities for payment
	Identify non-conformance issues with construction work and help develop potential resolution of non-conformance issues
	May serve as the lead Company representative at sub-sites where the Construction Site Manager or Construction Site Lead typically does not reside
	Provide construction core competency expertise when assigned to the function
	Ensure good communications and relations with the contractors


SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS




	Experience in-field Construction planning, execution, and completions
	Willing to travel domestic/overseas to project sites
	Past Construction Superintendent experience on major capital project
	Familiar with Construction Materials Testing procedures and ASTM standards
	Enthusiastic, self-starter with desire to provide excellent service to clients.

job_title: Senior HRBP/Performance, Reward & HR Services Manager M/F
description:
Company Description
Bourbon Interoil Nigeria Limited operates a modern fleet that meets the rigorous demands of the oil and gas industry. Specializing in crew-boats, including FSIVs for emergency supplies and Surfers for staff transportation between platforms, the company prioritizes safety and efficiency. With over 50 Nigerian-flagged and manned vessels, a dedicated Surfer Training Center equipped with a simulator, and compliance with all NCDMB requirements, Bourbon Interoil is committed to delivering safe, reliable, and cost-effective solutions.
The company is rooted in fostering sustainable relationships with stakeholders, guided by a strong Code of Conduct and adherence to all applicable laws and regulations.


Role Description
This is a full-time, on-site role based in Port Harcourt for a Senior HRBP/Performance, Reward & HR Services Manager. The individual in this role will be responsible for designing and implementing HR policies, managing employee relations, ensuring compliance with labor and employment laws, overseeing performance management processes, and leading strategic HR initiatives. The role includes providing strategic support to leadership, fostering positive employee experiences, and ensuring alignment with organizational goals.

Experience in Human Resources (HR) management and a deep understanding of HR PoliciesStrong skills in Employee Relations and a thorough knowledge of Labor and Employment LawExpertise in developing and implementing Performance Management strategiesExcellent decision-making, problem-solving, and leadership competenciesAbility to work effectively in a fast-paced, dynamic environmentBachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Human Resources, or a related field; advanced degree or certifications such as CIPM, SHRM, or CIPD is an advantagePrevious experience in the oil and gas or maritime industry is a plus

Savannah Energy is a young, high growth, highly ambitious energy company. We are focused on operating in countries where we can make a difference to local communities, where we can play a strategic role in the energy sector and where we can make a positive impact on the socio-economic development of the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:


Job Position: Contracts and Procurement Advisor


Job Location: Lagos
Job Purpose/Objectives 
The supply chain specialist is responsible for managing end-to-end supply chain activities, including procurement, inventory management, logistics coordination, and supplier relationship management. The role ensures cost efficiency, timely delivery of goods and services, and compliance with organisational policies, quality standards, and industry regulations.

Duties & Responsibilities

Manage procurement process for goods and services.
Solicit bids from vendors. Prepare Request for Quotes (“RFQs”) and issue to prospective vendors. Review vendor responses for compliance with requirements, perform pricing analysis, and recommend contract awards that result in best value procurement for assigned purchase requisitions.
Draft and manage contracts and procurement documentation.
Advise internal stakeholders on procurement strategies and performance.
Coordinate with internal stakeholders to ensure compliance and efficiency.
Source, negotiate and manage supplier relationships.
Maintain accurate records and ensure alignment with company policies and systems (e.g., SAP).
Support cost-saving initiatives and continuous process improvement.
Ensure that Outline Agreements are in place for all contracts as assigned.
Maintain and manage the filing (electronic shared drive and physical) of all supply chain documents in a chronological and systematic manner.
Work with requesters to match the purchase order and forward to the Accounts Department for payment. Resolve invoice discrepancies to ensure accurate accounts payable records are maintained.
Keep track of delivery dates and inspection status. Work with logistics agents and expeditors to ensure smooth movement of materials.
Maintain awareness of new trends and proactively stay appraised of new products or vendors.
Perform other duties as assigned by the Line Manager.

Job & Main Specifications (Education/Knowledge, Skills, Attributes, Experience & Other Required Details)

Bachelor’s Degree in Supply Chain Management, Engineering, Law or a related field.
At least eight years’ experience in supply chain, procurement or operations analytics; oil and gas experience preferred.
Strong knowledge of procurement processes, contract management and logistics operations.
Sound knowledge of Nigeria oil & gas industry procurement disciplines (Purchasing, Expediting, Inspection, and Shipping).
Knowledge of local and international markets, laws, rules and regulations for purchasing contracts and work contracts.
Excellent analytical, negotiation and problem-solving skills.
Strategic, analytical thinking, strong organisational and planning skills.
Strong understanding of supply chain processes, procurement lifecycles and inventory management best practices.
Proficiency in ERP systems (e.g., SAP) and MS Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
Be willing to live our values and have a passion for "Projects that Matter".

Note: Only qualified candidates will be contacted.

Job Responsibilities
 Source and acquire goods and services essential for oil and gas operations, ensuring cost-effectiveness and compliance with quality standards and regulations specific to the Nigerian oil and gas sector and Port Harcourt terrain. Collaborate closely with supply chain, production, and engineering teams to translate forecast plans into production schedules, maintain optimal inventory levels, and minimize material shortages, especially considering the local logistical challenges of Port Harcourt. Monitor and evaluate supplier performance, maintain updated supplier lists, and ensure timely delivery of materials, adapting to the unique supply chain and terrain conditions in Port Harcourt. Maintain accurate procurement records, including purchase histories, contracts, and inventory data, ensuring transparency and audit readiness. Conduct market research and vendor evaluations to identify cost-saving opportunities without compromising quality or compliance with industry standards such as ISO. Ensure adherence to safety, health, environmental policies, and legal procurement regulations relevant to the oil and gas industry in Nigeria. Manage risks related to supply chain disruptions by developing contingency plans tailored to the operational environment of Port Harcourt. Drive goods to client warehouse when necessary; Driving is required for this role and the interested candidate must have a valid drivers license and be experienced in driving.  Requirements
 Candidates should possess relevant qualifications. Driving Experience & License is Required.

**About Us**


NoemDek is an EPC firm incorporated in Nigeria in 2012, to provide specialized services to the oil and gas, power generation, FMCGs etc. Our primary focus is to employ our specialized, technical expertise and motivate our team of professionals in contributing positively to the Nigerian petroleum and energy main industry in particular. We are a powerhouse of technology, creativity, and expertise.

 **Primary Duties**  

* Provide efficient and effective driving support services for the company, ensuring safe transportation from various destinations and assist on entry and exit from the vehicle
* Develop and communicate transportation policies and procedures
* Provide appropriate solutions to and adjust schedule in response to unplanned event.
* Perform Airport pick\-up and point to point duties for the companies and clients
* Plan route and requirements by studying schedule requests by the Office
* Ensure all movements are neatly documented in the log attached to the car
* Keep up a travel log to record areas travelled to, travel time, and work hours
* Send weekly report on general activities on transportation operations.
* Including any other additional roles as directed by your supervisor or his representative

**Vehicle Maintenance**

* Monitor and communicate to the management through your supervisor the appropriate time for car check / Servicing.
* Perform required checklist on car daily as shall be provided by the company
* Renew Documents and ensure all documents required by the State and Federal Government are up\-to\-date
* Develop strategies to monitor and measure the effectiveness of the car.
* Ensure that the assigned vehicle is compliant with the Nigerian Federal Road Safety Corps operating requirements
* Ensure vehicle is kept clean and in good running conditions at all times
* Whenever the car is in your custody, ensure 100% safety of the car. In case of accident involving the car, put a call across to your supervisor before negotiating with the third party. All negotiations must be done in a professional manner

**Requirement / Skill set required**

  

* Minimum of 3 \- 5 years of driving experience in Lagos
* Valid Federal Government Driver’s License
* Defensive Driving
* Effective communication in the English Language (Spoken and Written)
* Positive attitude to work Neat and Professional Appearance
* Knowledge of driving Rules, Regulations, and courtesies
* Conversant with the Lagos Road Network and Traffic Regulations of Nigeria (Ikoyi, Lekki and Victoria Island axis are very important)
* Focused, Confident, and observant

**Business Development Executive (Oil \& Gas)**

We are seeking a high\-performing Business Development Executive to join our team in Victoria Island, Lagos. The ideal candidate will possess a deep network within the Oil and Gas sector and a proven track record of securing major contracts with International Oil Companies (IOCs).

**Key Responsibilities**

* Market Expansion: Identify and penetrate new client accounts and decision\-makers specifically within the Oil and Gas industry.

Industry Knowledge: Solid understanding of the Oil and Gas upstream sector, including experience with feasibility reports and tender preparations.

**Employment Details**

Type: Full\-time.

Premium Human Resources Solution \- Our client, a leading integrated facilities management and engineering services company in Nigeria, delivering world\-class solutions across oil \& gas, and industrial sectors.


They are recruiting to fill the position below:  

  

**Job Position: Product Quality Controller**


**Job Location:** Victoria Island, Lagos  

Employment Type: Full\-time


**Role Summary**  



  

* The Product Quality Controller is responsible for ensuring that all products, materials, and services delivered meet defined quality standards, client specifications, and regulatory requirements.
* The role involves systematic inspection, testing, documentation, and reporting throughout the product lifecycle \- from procurement and production through to final delivery.
* The ideal candidate is detail\-oriented, analytical, and committed to continuous quality improvement.


**Key Responsibilities**  

Quality Inspection \& Control:


* Conduct thorough inspections of incoming materials, in\-process products, and finished goods against approved specifications and standards.
* Perform physical, dimensional, and functional testing of products using appropriate tools and instruments.
* Identify, document, and escalate non\-conforming products; initiate and track corrective and preventive actions (CAPA).
* Monitor and verify that production processes comply with established Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and quality control plans.


Documentation \& Reporting:


* Maintain accurate quality records including inspection reports, test results, non\-conformance reports (NCRs), and quality logs.
* Prepare periodic quality performance reports with trend analysis and recommendations for management review.
* Ensure all documentation is in line with ISO standards and internal quality management system (QMS) requirements.


Standards \& Compliance:


* Ensure adherence to relevant industry standards (e.g., ISO 9001, SON, NAFDAC, client\-specific standards) across all quality activities.
* Support internal and external audits by providing required documentation and process walk\-throughs.
* Stay current with updates to quality regulations, codes, and best practices.


Continuous Improvement:


* Participate in root cause analysis (RCA) investigations for quality failures and contribute to the development of effective corrective measures.


**Qualifications \& Experience**  

Education:


* Bachelor's Degree or HND in Engineering (Mechanical, Chemical, Electrical), Sciences, or a closely related field.


Experience:


* 3–5 years of experience in a quality control or quality assurance role, preferably within engineering, manufacturing, oil \& gas, or facilities management.
* Demonstrated experience conducting quality inspections, managing NCRs, and implementing corrective actions.


Certifications (Preferred):


* ISO 9001: 2015 Lead Auditor or Internal Auditor certification.
* Certified Quality Inspector (CQI) or equivalent professional quality certification.

Our client is a group of companies in Nigeria. Due to expansion, they are in need of a **HEAD, QHSE**.

**Location:** Victoria Island, Lagos

**Job Responsibilities:**

* Lead programs to prevent uncontrolled releases of fuel, gas, or chemicals from refineries, storage tanks, pipelines, and retail tank systems.
* Review and update plans for managing major hazards such as fires, explosions, or toxic releases, in line with local safety regulations.
* Oversee the inspection and testing of critical safety devices including pressure relief valves, emergency shutdown systems, and shear valves on retail dispensers.
* Ensure that refined products such as gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel meet required industry specifications through regular sampling and testing.
* Manage quality checks during product transfer operations between storage tanks and into trucks, rail cars, or ships to avoid contamination.
* Investigate any off\-specification product events and coordinate corrective actions such as re\-blending, recall, or safe disposal.
* Enforce safe work procedures for high\-risk tasks including tank cleaning, confined space entry, and hot work such as welding on or near fuel lines.
* Control contractor safety for routine work like truck loading, tank maintenance, and pipe repairs through a formal permit system.
* Implement specific safety measures for loading and unloading operations, including vapor recovery, static electricity grounding, and emergency shutoffs.
* Ensure all fueling equipment (pumps, hoses, breakaway couplings, vapor recovery nozzles) is inspected daily for leaks, damage, or tampering at retail sites.
* Oversee emergency stop button functionality and shear valve testing to automatically cut fuel flow during a vehicle impact or fire.
* Manage forecourt traffic flow, speed limits, and bollard placement to prevent vehicle\-structure collisions or pedestrian injuries.
* Schedule regular leak detection testing using automatic tank gauging or inventory reconciliation on all underground fuel tanks at retail stations.
* Manage corrosion protection systems for steel tanks and piping to prevent gradual fuel releases into soil or groundwater.
* Establish safe procedures for tanker drivers during product delivery, including wheel chocks, grounding cables, and inspecting fill ports before and after each fuel drop.
* Maintain spill prevention and response plans for fuel storage areas, transfer zones, drainage systems, and retail forecourts, including regular practice drills.
* Monitor and report air emissions from process units, storage tank vents, and loading operations to stay within environmental permit limits.
* Ensure proper disposal of hydrocarbon\-contaminated waste such as sludge from tank bottoms and used filters and oversee groundwater monitoring wells at all locations.
* Develop and practice realistic emergency scenarios including storage tank fires, loading rack fires, retail forecourt fires, and gas releases.
* Maintain and test emergency systems such as foam deluge, gas detection, public address alarms, emergency isolation valves, and retail fire extinguishers.
* Act as the lead for incident command during on\-site emergencies, managing response teams and coordinating with local fire services.
* Deliver role\-specific safety training for refinery operators, laboratory staff, retail cashiers, tanker drivers, and contractors on topics such as lock\-out tag\-out, defensive driving, and spill response.
* Lead investigation teams for serious events such as fires, major spills, injuries, or customer incidents at retail sites using structured methods to find root causes.
* Encourage reporting of small leaks, seal failures, near misses, and retail hazards to prevent major accidents and share lessons learned across all sites.
* Maintain all site\-specific licenses and permits including fire department approvals, hazardous waste handling authorizations, and retail operating permits.
* Conduct unannounced QHSE audits of terminals, and retail outlets using checklists covering all operational areas and track corrective actions to closure.
* Prepare monthly safety, quality, and environmental reports for leadership, highlighting compliance status, incident trends, and priorities such as safety\-critical maintenance.

**Job Requirements:**

* Minimum of a first degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Chemical, Petroleum, or Industrial), Environmental Science, Occupational Health and Safety, or any related discipline.
* 8 – 10 years of previous experience in a QHSE role within the downstream oil and gas sector (refinery, terminal, depot, or retail service stations).
* Proven track record in managing process safety, environmental compliance, and occupational health across multiple site types.
* In\-depth knowledge of downstream operations including fuel storage, product transfer, truck loading, retail forecourt safety, and underground tank integrity.
* Strong leadership and people management skills, with experience supervising QHSE teams and influencing site managers.
* Credible experience in managing QHSE across supply value\-chains from refinery to retail.
* Experienced at setting and following strict budgets for QHSE programs, including spill response equipment, training, and audit schedules.
* Experience in implementing and overseeing relevant reporting and tracking systems for incidents, audits, inspections, and corrective actions.
* Expert knowledge and use of Microsoft Office Suite (MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.) for reporting, data analysis, and presentations.
* NMDPRA\-certified professional with deep experience building structures from scratch.

Work Location: In person

Our client is a group of companies in Nigeria. Due to expansion, they are in need of a **CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER.**

**Location:** Victoria Island, Lagos

**Job Objective**   
The Chief Operating Officer (COO) will provide end\-to\-end operational leadership across our client company, driving excellence across retail stations, commercial operations, supply chain, logistics, and asset management.

The role will be responsible for translating the Company’s strategic ambitions into scalable, efficient, and technology\-enabled operations, ensuring optimal performance across the downstream value chain. The COO will play a critical role in building a high\-performance, data\-driven, and customer\-centric organization, while driving cost efficiency, revenue growth, operational discipline, and continuous transformation.
