Work Location: In person

We are looking for a Fraud Manager - West Africa to join our Fraud team; as we scale up and drive digital and financial inclusion across our markets.

Role Overview

As Fraud Manager, you'll own the full cycle of consumer fraud risk management from establishing prevention systems and processes, to leading analytical detection and diagnosis of fraud events, to directing investigations and criminal proceedings, to implementing mitigation measures. You'll develop country-wide fraud policies and processes, manage KPIs, lead a team of investigators and analysts, build detection solutions, and collaborate with Legal, Internal Audit, and Product teams. You'll create a culture of fraud awareness across M-KOPA while managing a large portfolio of investigation and assurance engagements. This role directly protects how 7 million customers experience financial trust.

About M-KOPA

M-KOPA is a financial technology company protecting financial access for 7 million customers across Africa. We've unlocked $2+ billion in credit for people who'd never accessed formal financial services. We've contributed $45 million in taxes to African economies, assembled 2 million smartphones in Kenya, and financed 4,000+ e-motorbikes; saving riders an average of $5.62 daily.

We're deliberately scaling our fraud management infrastructure globally. We need leaders who understand that managing fraud risk here means protecting something different: 7 million customers accessing financial services for the first time, building trust in a system designed for their economic empowerment.

This is a hybrid role, you would be working from our offices in Nigeria, partnering with a diverse group of employees from our different markets and locations across the UK, Europe and Africa. You will be reporting to the Global Fraud Manager.

What You'll Do

Develop and maintain fraud prevention and management policies and processes. Lead end-to-end standardization of fraud risk identification and detection processes.

Assess fraud risk scenarios and design detection solutions integrating technologies and management expertise.

Lead and manage a team of field investigators and data analysts in prevention, detection, investigation, and mitigation of fraudulent activities.

Manage KPIs and drive accountability for cost-effective achievement of team objectives. Initiate new prevention and monitoring methods to improve the company's ability to foresee systemic risks.

Ensure data-driven decisions and professional standards of quality. Support Legal and Internal Audit in building criminal investigations, disciplinary processes, and systems that identify risks and trends.

Create fraud awareness culture through formal employee training. Coordinate insurance claims with Finance. Identify control gaps and recommend enhancements.

What You Bring

Minimum 5+ years of demonstrable experience in fraud risk prevention and detection—through fraud management, fraud analytics, internal audit, forensic investigations, or criminal justice in banking, payment, capital markets, insurance, FinTech, or energy sectors.

Bachelor's Degree in Criminology or related field.

Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE) credential or comparable fraud-related certification.

Advanced analytical capability - proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, Word, SharePoint, and PowerBI or data visualization systems. Strong comfort with data, analytical thinking, causal diagnosis, and trend interpretation.

Team and project leadership - ability to manage field-based operational teams and desk-based analytical resources. Strong background in cost-effective decision-making including ROI analysis and KPI monitoring.

Strategic fraud expertise - understanding of criminal justice systems, identification of fraud indicators through data mining, and ability to manage stakeholder engagement across diverse audiences.

Why M-KOPA?

At M-KOPA, we empower our people to own their careers through diverse development programs, coaching partnerships, and on-the-job training. We support individual journeys with family-friendly policies, prioritize well-being, and embrace flexibility.

Join us in shaping the future of M-KOPA as we grow together. Explore more at m-kopa.com.

Recognized four times by the Financial Times as one Africa's fastest growing companies (2022, 2023, 2024 and 2025) and by TIME100 Most influential companies in the world 2023 and 2024 , we've served over 6 million customers, unlocking $1.5 billion in cumulative credit for the unbanked across Africa.

M-KOPA explicitly prohibits the use of Forced or Child Labour and respects the rights of its employees to agree to terms and conditions of employment voluntarily, without coercion, and freely terminate their employment on appropriate notice. M-KOPA shall ensure that its Employees are of legal working age and shall comply with local laws for youth employment or student work, such as internships or apprenticeships.

M-KOPA does not collect/charge any money as a pre-employment or post-employment requirement. This means that we never ask for ‘recruitment fees’, ‘processing fees’, ‘interview fees’, or any other kind of money in exchange for offer letters or interviews at any time during the hiring process.

Applications for this position will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Shortlisting and interviews will take place at any stage during the recruitment process. We reserve the right to close the vacancy early if a suitable candidate is selected before the advertised closing date.

If your application is successful M-KOPA undertakes pre-employment background checks as part of its recruitment process, these include; criminal records, identification verification, academic qualifications, employment dates and employer references.

Job summary

The business development & market access manager will lead Nigeria-based delivery of ETK’s commercial acceleration, market development, and client management with a specific mandate for business research and data in line with specific client needs, with Nigeria designated as the priority market. The role is responsible for driving market access, pipeline development, and stakeholder engagement across the Oil & Gas, Extractives, Solid Minerals, Renewable Energy, Financial Service, Agriculture and Heritage/Film sectors. Working closely with ETK leadership and commercial and technical teams, the role will ensure that agreed activities translate into qualified leads, executive-level conversations, and sustained engagement momentum. This is a hands-on, execution-focused, client-facing role, operating between strategy and delivery, with responsibility for managing workstreams, coordinating stakeholders, and tracking progress against agreed KPIs.

 Min Qualification: Degree Experience Level: Senior level Experience Length: 5 years
Job descriptions & requirements
Responsibilities:
Business Development & Pipeline Coordination (Nigeria Priority):

Identify, prioritise, and manage high-value private-sector target accounts in Nigeria across target sectors including oil & gas, extractives and solid minerals, heritage, archives, and film-related institutions, renewable energy, financial services and agriculture
Support persona-led outreach aligned to regulatory timelines, asset transitions, and data-modernisation needs.
Maintain a structured and prioritised commercial pipeline, with a strong emphasis on Nigeria-based opportunities.
Progress qualified engagements from initial contact to executive-level conversations in line with agreed milestones.

Market Access & Stakeholder Engagement:
Act as the day-to-day delivery interface for ongoing project engagements on Nigeria-related activities.
Engage Nigerian regulators, government ministries and agencies, industry bodies, chambers of commerce, and ecosystem partners to facilitate access and introductions.
Coordinate executive-level introductions between the client's/partner's organisation leadership and senior decision-makers.
Represent ETK and its clients professionally in high-level meetings and curated engagements.

Proposal Development & Sales:
Lead in the development of business proposals and responses to RFP’s on business opportunities within target sectors.
Develop a business plan and sales strategy for the target market, ensuring attainment with company sales and profitability objectives.
Create and conduct proposal presentations at high-level engagements with prospects and RFP responses.
Drive market monitoring and track global business trends to identify potential business opportunities.

Event Representation & Lead Nurturing:

Support representation at flagship oil and gas and extractive events in Nigeria.
Capture, qualify, and prioritise event-generated leads based on strategic fit and commercial readiness.
Ensure timely and structured post-event follow-up to convert contacts into qualified opportunities.

Regional Coordination (Ghana & Angola):
Support targeted outreach and engagement activities in Ghana and Angola in coordination with ETK leadership.
Assist with lead qualification, meeting coordination, and reporting for regional markets.
Coordinate with regional associates to ensure regional activities align with overall pipeline and KPI targets.

Delivery Management & Reporting:
Coordinate delivery across the three agreed workstreams: private-sector lead development, event representation, Institutional and regulatory penetration
Track performance against KPIs, including: An agreed number of qualified private-sector leads per month, Event-sourced leads per month (subject to event calendar), Institutional or regulatory introduction per month
Produce clear monthly activity summaries and progress reports.
Proactively identify risks, dependencies, and delivery constraints, escalating where required.

Scope & Role Boundaries:
The role facilitates market access, introductions, and engagement progression.
Responsibility for technical scoping, commercial negotiation, and contract closure remains with clients, in line with the Statement of Work.
Success is measured by pipeline quality, velocity, and stakeholder access, not closed revenue.

Requirements:
Strong business development and pipeline coordination capability
5–7 years’ experience in business development support, market access, consulting, or professional services delivery
Nigeria-based, with demonstrable experience operating in Nigerian commercial or institutional settings
Proven experience supporting senior-level engagements and structured client mandates
Exposure to Africa-focused or multi-country projects
Confident client-facing communication with senior stakeholders
Stakeholder mapping and relationship management
High level of organisation, follow-through, and execution discipline
Clear written and verbal reporting skills

Nigeria-Specific Competencies:
Deep familiarity with Nigeria’s Oil & Gas, extractives, renewable energy or regulated enterprise environments
Comfort engaging with Nigerian regulators, MDAs, and industry bodies
Strong understanding of how commercial and institutional decisions are made in Nigeria

Desirable Experience:
Existing relationships within Nigeria’s Oil & gas, extractives, renewable energy, financial services or infrastructure ecosystems.
Experience supporting international firms entering or expanding in Nigeria
Exposure to Ghana or Angola markets
Event representation or industry conference experience

Critical Success Factors:
Strong local credibility and professional presence in Nigeria
Ability to convert introductions into sustained engagement momentum
Consistent tracking and reporting against KPIs
High responsiveness to client and stakeholder needs
Strong partnership skills and ability to identify business opportunities.

Job summary

description:
About the Role:

Grade Level (for internal use): 09 Grade/Level : 9
The Location: Abuja, Nigeria
The Role: Instructor, Energy Industry Fundamentals Training (French Speaker)

The Team: SPGE offers cross-commodity industry fundamentals training to energy majors and top chemical producers globally. After a couple of years of expansion, our training catalogue currently includes Oil, LNG, Power and Energy Expansion Industry Fundamentals.
Building on the success of our well-stablished energy & chemicals training portfolio, we would like to expand further our training business, particularly in West Africa.
The Impact: You will contribute initially to the winning, renewal and execution of Energy related training projects globally. Projects typically leverage S&P Global Energy data, research and expertise and cover topics including, industry fundamentals, price forecasting and trading strategies.
What’s in it for you: You will work with subject matter experts across the organization to originate and produce high-quality training courses and manage resources as required. During your projects, you will get exposure to market leaders globally and experts at all levels of major oil and gas and chemical companies.
Responsibilities:

Develop and deliver state of the art training, working with the lead content matter experts within the organization. This will typically require:

Participate in meetings with clients to understand training requirements

Identify internal and external information sources, gather, and synthesize data

Efficiently analyze data to produce training materials

Produce training materials, for clients

Present training materials to the clients.

Follow-up regarding further training needs
What We’re Looking For:

Experience within the energy industry, and a deep understanding of the oil & gas value chain.

Engineering background is required.

Fluency in both French and English is essential. The candidate must possess excellent verbal and written communication skills in both languages

We welcome applications from citizens of Nigeria and citizens of other ECOWAS member states; selection is subject to verification of citizenship and work eligibility.

An analytical frame of mind with ability to use data to back up opinions.

Ability to work well in a team-oriented environment providing ideas and open feedback.

Willingness to travel, average around 20-40% of time (could occasionally be more for specific projects)
Please be advised that our company will verify all information included in your resume and application via a formal background check verification process in accordance with applicable law. Providing false or misleading information may mean that we cannot accept you as a candidate or may lead to termination of future employment.

About S&P Global Energy
At S&P Global Energy, our comprehensive view of global energy and commodities markets enables our customers to make superior decisions and create long-term, sustainable value. Our four core capabilities are: Platts for news and pricing; CERA for research and advisory; Horizons for energy expansion and sustainability solutions; and Events for industry collaboration.

S&P Global Energy is a division of S&P Global (NYSE: SPGI). S&P Global enables businesses, governments, and individuals with trusted data, expertise, and technology to make decisions with conviction. We are Advancing Essential Intelligence through world-leading benchmarks, data, and insights that customers need in order to plan confidently, act decisively, and thrive economically in a rapidly changing global landscape. Learn more at www.spglobal.com/energy .

What’s In It For You?

Our Mission:
Advancing Essential Intelligence.

Our People:
We're more than 35,000 strong worldwide—so we're able to understand nuances while having a broad perspective. Our team is driven by curiosity and a shared belief that Essential Intelligence can help build a more prosperous future for us all.From finding new ways to measure sustainability to analyzing energy transition across the supply chain to building workflow solutions that make it easy to tap into insight and apply it. We are changing the way people see things and empowering them to make an impact on the world we live in. We’re committed to a more equitable future and to helping our customers find new, sustainable ways of doing business. Join us and help create the critical insights that truly make a difference.

Our Values:
Integrity, Discovery, Partnership

Throughout our history, the world's leading organizations have relied on us for the Essential Intelligence they need to make confident decisions about the road ahead. We start with a foundation of integrity in all we do, bring a spirit of discovery to our work, and collaborate in close partnership with each other and our customers to achieve shared goals.

Benefits:
We take care of you, so you can take care of business. We care about our people. That’s why we provide everything you—and your career—need to thrive at S&P Global.

Our benefits include:

Health & Wellness: Health care coverage designed for the mind and body.

Flexible Downtime: Generous time off helps keep you energized for your time on.

Continuous Learning: Access a wealth of resources to grow your career and learn valuable new skills.

Invest in Your Future: Secure your financial future through competitive pay, retirement planning, a continuing education program with a company-matched student loan contribution, and financial wellness programs.

Family Friendly Perks: It’s not just about you. S&P Global has perks for your partners and little ones, too, with some best-in class benefits for families.

Beyond the Basics: From retail discounts to referral incentive awards—small perks can make a big difference.
For more information on benefits by country visit: https://spgbenefits.com/benefit-summaries

Global Hiring and Opportunity at S&P Global:
At S&P Global, we are committed to fostering a connected and engaged workplace where all individuals have access to opportunities based on their skills, experience, and contributions. Our hiring practices emphasize fairness, transparency, and merit, ensuring that we attract and retain top talent. By valuing different perspectives and promoting a culture of respect and collaboration, we drive innovation and power global markets.

Job Position: Senior Project Engineer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Description

We are seeking a highly qualified Senior Project Engineer to join our team.
As a Senior Project Engineer, you will play a crucial role in overseeing operating processes used to complete projects and check that staff members perform their duties to a high standard to ensure the project runs smoothly, oversee the needs of the client, and find ways to improve processes.
To plan, organise, and control all elements of the project and ensure the requirements of contracts are being satisfied within the stipulated budget and timeframe.

Main Responsibilities

Responsible for scheduling, conducting, and coordinating the technical and managerial aspects of projects.
Prepare, coordinate, and monitor a project from the initial conception to the schedule and final completion whether onshore, offshore, or swamp.
Involved with the planning and forecasting of budgets and timelines to assure that the project is accurately completed to schedule.
Assign tasks and responsibilities to members of the team so that everything is completed on time. This involves mentoring the project team and managing its performance.
Ensure accurate and timely reporting standards are maintained throughout the project(s).
Monitoring project status reports and change orders.
Perform quality control checks throughout all stages of the schedule to ensure budgets are kept, deadlines are met, and the performance of the team is as it should be.
Comply with quality policy in line with ISO 9001:2015 standard.
Ensure safety and QA/QC programs are established by all parties and maintained, and conduct reviews of detailed design drawings and specifications.
Accountable for the successful completion of engineering projects.
Participate in client/contractor meetings and resolve client, engineering, and management project issues.
Identify engineering problems and ensure solutions are implemented.
Evaluate, organise, and prioritise workload within a schedule.
Liaise with third-party contractors to ensure all equipment is fit for purpose.
Head the site HSE meeting/discussion and attend the morning pep talk before the start of work.
Attend safety meetings when programmed.
Investigate and give first-hand information/details on all accidents on the site.
Conduct checks on personal protective equipment and encourage their use.
Head the incident investigation team.
Wear appropriate PPE on site.
Stop any work that can potentially harm people, assets, or the environment.
Be responsible for the protection of lives and assets of the company.

Qualifications / Requirements

B.Sc or HND in Mechanical Engineering.
Experience: 5 - 8 years of hands-on experience as a Project Engineer working in the oil and gas industry.
Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria (COREN) membership.
PMP certification is an added advantage.
HSE Level 3 minimum.
Strong communication and leadership skills.
Work experience in offshore environments.
Should reside in Port Harcourt or be willing to relocate.

CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System or Software).
Microsoft Office Project.
Primavera.
Strong track record of successfully completed projects.
Excellent Microsoft Office skills.
Ability to use own initiative.
The ability to explain design ideas and plans clearly.
Confident decision-making ability.
Excellent communication skills both written and oral.
The ability to work within budgets and to deadlines.
A comprehensive knowledge of relevant legal regulations.
Good team-leading skills.

description:
Job Description

Full time

Your mission

SPIE Services Nigeria Limited is currently looking for High Profile Contract Engineer (Expat) to join its work force in Nigeria.

Responsibilities shall include:

Verify that company's Contractual Policies and applicable laws and regulations are adhered to, especially related to ethics and transparency.

Role: Managing Director Downstream Operations

Location: Lagos (Nigeria)

Reports to: CEO

Industry: Oil & Gas

Overview:

Our client is a prominent Oil and Gas exploration and production and marketing company headquartered in Nigeria, seeking a dynamic and results-driven Managing Director to oversee and lead their Downstream operations. This role involves managing the sales and distribution of lubricants, overseeing a network of petrol stations and driving customer-focused initiatives that ensure market leadership in the industry. The ideal candidate will have significant experience in managing downstream operations, particularly in the FMCG sector, with a deep understanding of retail, brand management, distribution networks and customer relations.

Key Responsibilities:

Leadership & Strategic Direction:

- Lead the development and execution of the companys downstream strategy, with a focus on growth in lubricant sales, retail petrol stations and expanding market share.

- Develop and implement innovative strategies to drive sales, improve market penetration, and enhance profitability in the downstream sector.

- Lead and inspire senior management and operational teams across sales, marketing, supply chain and retail operations to achieve business objectives and align with corporate goals.

- Represent the company in high-level external engagements, including partnerships, industry forums, regulatory bodies and media, ensuring alignment with the companys interests and values.

Lubricants Business Management:

- Oversee the full lifecycle of the lubricants business, including product development, marketing, sales, distribution and customer service.

- Drive brand positioning and marketing efforts to establish and maintain a competitive edge in the lubricants market.

- Build and maintain strong relationships with key distributors, wholesalers and retailers to drive penetration and expand the companys reach in both domestic and regional markets.

- Optimize the product mix and pricing strategies to maximize revenue and market share while maintaining competitive advantage.

- Monitor customer feedback and market trends to continuously improve product offerings and customer experience.

Retail & Petrol Stations Management:

- Oversee the operations of a network of retail petrol stations across Nigeria, ensuring that all stations meet the companys standards for quality, safety and customer service.

- Implement strategies to drive fuel and non-fuel sales, improving profitability at each station while maintaining brand integrity and customer loyalty.

Sales & Distribution:

- Oversee the nationwide distribution network for lubricants and fuel, ensuring timely and efficient delivery to customers while minimizing operational costs.

- Drive sales targets for lubricants and fuel, with a particular focus on expanding the retail network and increasing the companys share of the market.

- Monitor sales performance, analyse data and adjust strategies to meet changing market conditions and consumer behaviour.

- Manage the logistics and supply chain for fuel and lubricant distribution, ensuring that inventory levels are optimally managed and demand forecasts are accurately met.

Qualifications & Experience:

Education: A Bachelors degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Engineering, or a related field. A Masters degree or MBA is highly preferred.

Experience: Minimum of 15 years of experience in the downstream oil and gas industry, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership role. Experience in managing lubricants sales, retail petrol station operations and FMCG distribution is essential.

Industry Knowledge: In-depth knowledge of downstream oil and gas operations, including fuel retail, lubricants product management and distribution networks.

FMCG Expertise: Proven experience in the FMCG sector, with expertise in brand management, customer relations, retail operations and sales distribution.

job_title: Commercial and CX Graduate Associate (Fluent French Speaker)
description:
About the Role:

Grade Level (for internal use): 09
The S&P Global Energy Commercial and CX Graduate Program journey begins with an intensive 5-week onboarding in our London office.

The 3-months thereafter will be part of the EMEA Customer Success Team , focusing on building a foundational knowledge of the commodity markets, developing essential business-related skills while learning about various S&P Global Energy data, research and analytical products. With Customer Success, you would be a primary contact for clients, providing solutions to their requests in a timely, professional and courteous manner and providing training on S&P Global Energy products through virtual and onsite visits.

The program will consist of rotations to customer success, sales, channel management and marketing functions . This will enable each graduate to gain a holistic perspective of the business. Combined with on-the-job learning, graduates must complete and pass certification milestones along with a rigorous evaluation process that will test and stretch their skills and capabilities. As you progress though the program, you will have the opportunity to support the sales team and ultimately generate additional revenue that helps to grow our business

You will be offered the opportunity to assist S&P Global Energy in achieving its mission of being one of the leading providers of the highest quality price assessments and analytical information to the world’s financial and commodity markets. Our real-time news, pricing and analytical services help markets operate with transparency and efficiency worldwide. As the principal provider of commodity price assessment and information to more than 10,000 customers in 150 countries and with 20 offices spanning five continents, you will form part of a truly globally diverse workforce. You will be part of a rapidly growing organization with an exceptional corporate culture. Our data, analytics, and research help our customers make informed, smarter business decisions and investments.

We are looking for high achievers from a diverse pool of current undergraduates or recent graduates who are passionate about the energy and commodities markets and want to develop their career in a dynamic, high performing and multicultural commercial team. You will be someone who can quickly work out how to make things happen through intellectual curiosity, creativity, exceptional learning agility and problem solving skills as well as effective networking and collaboration skills. Additionally, proficiency in Generative AI technologies and the ability to leverage these tools for innovative solutions will be highly valued.

What’s in it for you:

You will join as a member of a cohort of Commercial and CX Associates. Here, you will be introduced to world-class experts in various commodities including former chief economists, traders and risk managers. They will give presentations about how commodity markets work and provide insightful perspectives on global economic, political, technological and legal issues linked to their specializations. You will also be enrolled in an e-learning curriculum, providing foundational knowledge about the technical aspects of various commodities and market.

Within weeks you will have the opportunity to present topics to senior management and to get constructive feedback on your progress. Throughout the program you will learn how to use tools including S&P Global Energy-Platts, CERA and HORIZON products, Excel add-ins and CRM systems.

You will be challenged on a daily basis with a steep learning curve. You will get the opportunity to work with a broad range of clients including governments, investment banks, commodity trading houses, major oil and gas companies, hedge funds and industry and get to be part of a large energetic and international team with fantastic opportunities for growth.

Career paths after the 18-month initial phase of the program include:

Regional Sales

Specialist Sales

New Business Sales

Customer Success

Marketing

Omni Channel Distribution / AI and Data feed

Responsibilities:

Help clients understand how to navigate and extract useful data from the S&P Global Energy product range.

Liaise with the content, commercial and technical teams to address any client queries and ensure our data is maintained to the highest standards.

Proactively reaching out to clients for face-to-face/virtual training (this will apply towards the later stages of development once fully fluent and certified on the product range).

Conduct research about important issues at the forefront of energy and commodity markets and present this to colleagues.

Support sales teams with research and proposals.

Build account overviews and plans to support strategic sales teams.

Design data-driven models and templates for clients.

Create marketing materials for commercial culture initiatives and future graduate cohorts.

Getting involved in special project initiatives where determined necessary by management.

Requirements:

Ability to join a class of new hires in late August 2026

BSc or BA degree, or equivalent.

Fluency in both French and English is essential. The candidate must possess excellent verbal and written communication skills in both languages to effectively engage with clients and stakeholders.

We welcome applications from citizens of Nigeria and citizens of other ECOWAS member states; selection is subject to verification of citizenship and work eligibility.

Strong presentation and communication skills. You must have good telephone manners and be comfortable presenting to large groups both in person and remotely.

Strong understanding and command of AI Capabilities.

Familiarity with or strong interest in learning about financial and commodity/ENERGY markets.

Good PC skills including the Microsoft Office product range.

Well versed in Excel

Ability to effectively multi-task and work independently.

Willingness to learn and take on a wide variety of tasks.

Please be advised that our company will verify all information included in your resume and application via a formal background check verification process in accordance with applicable law. Providing false or misleading information may mean that we cannot accept you as a candidate or may lead to termination of future employment.

About S&P Global Energy
At S&P Global Energy, our comprehensive view of global energy and commodities markets enables our customers to make superior decisions and create long-term, sustainable value. Our four core capabilities are: Platts for news and pricing; CERA for research and advisory; Horizons for energy expansion and sustainability solutions; and Events for industry collaboration.

S&P Global Energy is a division of S&P Global (NYSE: SPGI). S&P Global enables businesses, governments, and individuals with trusted data, expertise, and technology to make decisions with conviction. We are Advancing Essential Intelligence through world-leading benchmarks, data, and insights that customers need in order to plan confidently, act decisively, and thrive economically in a rapidly changing global landscape. Learn more at www.spglobal.com/energy .

What’s In It For You?

Our Mission:
Advancing Essential Intelligence.

Our People:
We're more than 35,000 strong worldwide—so we're able to understand nuances while having a broad perspective. Our team is driven by curiosity and a shared belief that Essential Intelligence can help build a more prosperous future for us all.From finding new ways to measure sustainability to analyzing energy transition across the supply chain to building workflow solutions that make it easy to tap into insight and apply it. We are changing the way people see things and empowering them to make an impact on the world we live in. We’re committed to a more equitable future and to helping our customers find new, sustainable ways of doing business. Join us and help create the critical insights that truly make a difference.

Posted On: 2026-03-25
Location: Abuja, Federal Capital, Nigeria

SENIOR ACCOUNTANT JOB DESCRIPTION

Our client is a recognised player in the upstream oil and gas sector, committed to delivering high-performance solutions that support efficient and safe field operations. With a strong focus on innovation and service excellence, the company specializes in the production and supply of high-quality chemicals, as well as comprehensive maintenance services for oil fields. They work closely with industry stakeholders to enhance operational productivity, extend asset life cycles, and ensure optimal performance across exploration and production activities.

Senior Accountant reporting to Finance Manager / CFO Lagos, Nigeria Full-time, Permanent and sector is Upstream Oil & Gas — Chemicals & Field Maintenance Experience Required 7 – 9 Years (incl. consulting background)

The Senior Accountant will play a pivotal role in strengthening internal controls, ensuring accurate and timely financial reporting, and bringing a consultative lens to financial analysis and cost management.

The client is specifically seeking someone with a consulting or advisory background, an individual who has worked beyond the transactional accounting function and is comfortable translating financial data into actionable insights for business leaders. This hire represents an investment in the quality and maturity of the company's financial operations.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Financial Reporting & Compliance

● Prepare accurate and timely monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements in line with IFRS and company policies.

● Ensure full compliance with Nigerian tax obligations including PAYE, VAT, WHT, CIT, and FIRS filings.

● Coordinate with external auditors and manage the statutory audit process end-to-end.

● Maintain the integrity of the general ledger, chart of accounts, and supporting documentation. Management Accounting & Advisory

● Prepare detailed management accounts, cost analyses, and financial performance reports for senior leadership.

● Conduct variance analysis, identifying root causes of budget deviations and recommending corrective action.

● Apply a consultative approach to financial analysis, framing insights in the context of operational and commercial implications.

● Support pricing decisions, project cost reviews, and contract evaluations from a financial perspective. Budgeting & Forecasting

● Lead the annual budgeting cycle and rolling forecast processes, working cross-functionally with operations, procurement, and commercial teams.

● Track financial performance against budget and provide regular updates and recommendations to leadership.

● Develop scenario models and financial projections to support strategic planning. Internal Controls & Process Improvement

● Review, document, and strengthen internal financial controls and accounting procedures. ● Identify process inefficiencies and propose improvements that reduce financial risk and improve accuracy.

● Ensure the appropriate use and integrity of the company's accounting software and ERP systems. Treasury & Working Capital

● Monitor cash flow, manage bank reconciliations, and ensure effective working capital management.

● Oversee accounts payable and receivable, ensuring timely processing and reconciliation. ● Liaise with banks and financial institutions as required.

QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE

● Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.

● Professional certification required: ACA (ICAN), ACCA, or equivalent. Candidates who are in the final stage of qualification will be considered.

● 5 to 8 years of progressive accounting experience, with a minimum of 2 years in a consulting, advisory, or professional services environment.

● Prior experience in the oil and gas sector or energy-related industries is a strong advantage.

● Proficiency in accounting software (e.g., Sage, QuickBooks, SAP, or similar ERP platforms). ● Advanced Excel skills; familiarity with data visualisation or financial modelling tools is a plus.

● Strong working knowledge of IFRS, Nigerian tax regulations, and relevant statutory requirements.

● Demonstrated ability to produce high-quality financial reports, management accounts, and board-ready presentations.

IDEAL CANDIDATE PROFILE

We are looking for a financially astute, analytically rigorous, and commercially aware accountant who brings more than technical competence to this role. You have spent meaningful time in a consulting or advisory environment, whether in a Big Four or mid-tier audit/advisory firm, a management consulting firm, or an in-house function that operated in a consultative capacity, and you understand how to translate numbers into decisions.

You are not a passive order-taker. You are a thought partner, someone who walks into a business and quickly diagnoses what the financials are telling them, identifies what is missing, and knows how to communicate that clearly to people who are not accountants. You are disciplined in execution but flexible in thinking, and you bring energy and standards to every output you produce. Your background in the oil and gas sector, whether upstream, midstream, or oilfield services, will be an advantage, but what matters most is your ability to operate in a technically complex, fast-moving environment where precision, adaptability, and good judgment are non-negotiable.

TRANSFORMATION OBJECTIVES

In the first 12 months, the successful candidate will be expected to:

● Establish a more disciplined month-end close process and ensure full compliance with IFRS and relevant Nigerian tax regulations.

● Lead a structured review of existing financial controls and identify gaps or inefficiencies in the company's accounting systems and processes.

● Develop cost analysis frameworks that give operational leadership better visibility into project profitability and overhead management.

● Bring consulting-quality thinking to budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis, producing outputs that drive commercial decisions, not just satisfy compliance requirements.

● Support the finance leadership team in building a finance function that can scale with the business over the next three to five years.

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: ₦500,000.00 - ₦800,000.00 per month

Work Location: In person

*Job Profile*

Vertical Project Nigeria Ltd is seeking an energetic and technically proficient Technical Sales Engineer to drive business growth within the Nigerian Oil & Gas sector. You will be the bridge between complex engineering solutions and our clients' commercial needs. This role requires a unique blend of "boots-on-the-ground" technical knowledge and high-level business development skills.

You will manage the full sales lifecycle—from identifying opportunities in FEED (Front-End Engineering Design) to providing after-sales support for critical equipment like Marine Loading Arms and Rotork Actuators. The successful candidate will not just sell products but will position Vertical Project Nigeria Ltd as a premier partner in installation, maintenance, and integrity testing.

*Key Responsibilities*

· Business Development: Identify and capture new business opportunities across the Upstream, Midstream, and Downstream sectors, focusing on equipment supply (Valves, Actuators, and Loading Arms) and servicing.

· Technical Consulting: Provide pre-sales technical assistance and product education. Demonstrate how our solutions (e.g., Emco Wheaton Marine Loading Arms or Rotork Actuators) satisfy client needs in terms of quality, price, and long-term reliability.

· Tenders & Bidding: Lead the technical portion of bids and tenders. Calculate accurate client quotations and negotiate contract terms that align with both client requirements and company profitability.

· Project Liaison: Collaborate with the Engineering and Installation teams to ensure seamless execution of site fabrications, piping, and electrical installations.

· Integrity & Compliance: Promote our specialized services, including Surface/Underground Tank Calibration and Tanker/Truck Integrity Testing, ensuring all proposals meet regulatory and safety standards.

· Relationship Management: Cultivate long-term relationships with IOCs, NOCs, and independent operators. Serve as the primary technical point of contact for after-sales support and troubleshooting.

· Market Intelligence: Conduct research on energy market trends and competitor activity to update marketing strategies and business plans.

*Technical Competencies*

· Fluid Transfer & Flow Control: Strong understanding of Marine Loading Arms, Valve types, and Actuation systems.

· Engineering Design: Familiarity with the stages of Project FEED, Detailed Engineering Design (DED), and Commissioning.

· Field Services: Knowledge of piping/structural steel fabrication and electrical installation/maintenance.

· Standards: Understanding of industry-specific regulations and occupational health and safety (HSE) protocols.

*Requirements & Qualifications*

· Education: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical/Electronic, or a related field).

· Experience: 3–5 years of proven experience in technical sales or business development within the Oil & Gas servicing sector.

· Product Knowledge: Direct experience with Emco Wheaton, Rotork, or similar high-spec OEMs is highly desirable.

· Sector Expertise: Solid background in the EPIC (Engineering, Procurement, Installation, and Commissioning) sector and familiarity with the Nigerian energy regulatory environment.

· Soft Skills:

o Excellent negotiation and "closing" skills.

o Analytical mind-set with the ability to solve complex technical problems.

o Resilience and the tenacity to manage long sales cycles.

o Exceptional written and verbal communication for high-level presentations.

Tools: Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite/Google Workspace and CRM software.

*What We Offer*

· Opportunity to work with industry-leading brands and technologies.

· A dynamic environment at the forefront of Nigeria's energy infrastructure development.

· Competitive performance-based incentives.

· HMO coverage.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: From ₦200,000.00 per month

* Are you familiar with Actuators, Motorised Valves and Loading Arms?

Education:
* Higher National Diploma (Preferred)

Experience:
* Technical Sales: 2 years (Preferred)
* Engineering: 2 years (Preferred)

*Business Development Executive (Oil & Gas)*

We are seeking a high-performing Business Development Executive to join our team in Victoria Island, Lagos. The ideal candidate will possess a deep network within the Oil and Gas sector and a proven track record of securing major contracts with International Oil Companies (IOCs).

*Key Responsibilities*

* Market Expansion: Identify and penetrate new client accounts and decision-makers specifically within the Oil and Gas industry.
* Contract Acquisition: Win major contracts across core service areas, including EPCIM (Engineering, Procurement, Construction, Installation & Maintenance) and Manpower Supply.
* Strategic Networking: Grow and leverage your professional network to meet potential clients and represent the company at technical meetings, workshops, and trade shows.
* Bid Management: Rigorously follow up on bids submitted to NAPIMS and other key clients.
* Partnership Management: Identify and manage strategic alliances with third-party technical partners to expand business capacity and technology leverage.
* Performance Tracking: Drive departmental objectives through rigorous tracking of KPIs and operational efficiencies.

*Requirements*

* Education: Minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree (BSc) in Tech, Social Sciences, or similar fields.
* Experience: A minimum of 6 years of professional experience.

Industry Knowledge: Solid understanding of the Oil and Gas upstream sector, including experience with feasibility reports and tender preparations.

Skills: Strong negotiation skills, strategic thinking, and the ability to articulate new business opportunities in line with corporate goals.

*Employment Details*

Type: Full-time.

Location: Victoria Island, Lagos, Nigeria.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: ₦1,200,000.00 - ₦1,500,000.00 per month

Work Location: In person

*Company:* Jawinek Services Nigeria Limited

*Location:* 100% Remote (Lagos)

*About The Job*

*Company Description*

JAWINEK SERVICES NIG LTD is a leading company specializing in Engineering, Procurement, Construction, Installation, Marine, and Logistics (EPCIML) services. Our diverse portfolio allows us to cater to various industries, offering comprehensive solutions that meet specific client needs. We are committed to excellence and innovation, ensuring high-quality service delivery in all our projects.

*Role Overview*

We are seeking a proactive and results-oriented Business Development Executive with a minimum of 5 years of professional experience to support the company’s growth by identifying new business opportunities, maintaining client relationships, and assisting in securing contracts. The ideal candidate will contribute to expanding the company’s market presence and increasing revenue. Experience in the Oil & Gas industry is highly preferred.

*Key Responsibilities*

* Identify and pursue new business opportunities to support company growth.
* Build and maintain strong relationships with existing and potential clients.
* Conduct market research to identify potential clients, projects, and partnership opportunities.
* Assist in preparing business proposals, quotations, and tender submissions.
* Support the negotiation and closing of business deals.
* Maintain and update the company’s client database and business development pipeline.
* Follow up on leads, inquiries, and ongoing opportunities to ensure conversion.
* Collaborate with internal teams to ensure smooth execution of awarded projects.
* Represent the company in meetings with clients and at industry networking events.
* Provide regular reports on business development activities and progress.

*Requirements*

* Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Engineering or a related field.
* Minimum of 5 years of professional experience in business development, sales or a related role.
* Experience in the Oil & Gas industry is preferred.
* Strong communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.
* Ability to identify opportunities and convert leads into business.
* Good organizational and analytical skills.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office and business reporting tools.
* Ability to work independently and meet targets.

*How to Apply*

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to *careers@jawinekservices.com* with the subject line:*“Business Development Executive – Jawinek Services.”*

*Note*: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. If you do not receive a response within 3 weeks of your application, please consider your application unsuccessful.

Job Type: Full-time

Work Location: Remote

*About Our Client*

Our client, a leading organization with three decades of experience in the marine industry; is a Nigerian owned and managed provider of marine logistics services to the Oil and Gas industry.

Our client own and operate a wide range of marine vessels servicing major International Oil Companies and have established partnerships with various reputable international vessel owners building a strong track record in providing various vessels in the Nigerian Oil & Gas industry. Our client has an ambition to expand their service offerings and significantly increase their market share by 2028.

*Job Objective*

· Ensures the delivery of effective financial decision-making support to the business and its management, and oversee the development and execution of an effective financial strategy that will ensure optimal returns on investments for the business

· Establishes an effective framework for accurate recording, controlling and reporting on the company’s financial transactions and facilitate the judicious use of its financial resources

· Supports business growth and ensure sustainable profitability by providing sound advice on the financial implications of growth plans.

*ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES*

· Formulates, updates and implements finance strategies, policies and processes

· Provides strategic financial management and leadership support across the business, in line with the overall corporate strategy

· Engage Executive management and the appropriate Board Committees to develop short, medium, and long-term financial plans and projections for the organization

· Develops and utilizes financial models and activity-based financial analysis to provide a basis for investment decisions and business planning

· Develops and manages key relationships with relevant external bodies / contacts e.g. regulatory organizations auditors, solicitors, banks etc. to facilitate effective business operations

· Advises management on financial benefits or demerits of proposed projects, changes in financial regulations and legislations as well as changes in competition and market trends.

· Raises funds as approved by the Board to finance our client’s activities

· Provides advice to executive management and the relevant Board Committee on the financial implications of business continuity plans

· Liaise with external and internal auditors; and ensure the annual and/or periodic audits are seamlessly carried out

· Manages our client’s treasury assets to achieve optimal sourcing, application and growth of funds

· Establishes appropriate and sound standards and procedures for internal and third-party payments across the business. Empowers and supports the Finance team to drive implementation and communication across the business

· Ensures adequate controls are in place to facilitate seamless payment and efficient financial management

· Develops and implements sound accounting framework in compliance with statutory provisions

· Develops and implements a robust and reliable financial reporting system in compliance with statutory requirements

· Establishes and implements the appropriate approval levels across Group Finance for recording and effecting accruals, adjustments (e.g. write-offs etc.)

· Approves payrolls along with the Head, Human Capital to facilitate payment to staff

· Oversees the preparation and communication of timely, accurate, and useful financial and management reports for investors/lending institutions and our client’s management on a periodic and /or ad-hoc basis

· Oversees the establishment of an effective debtor management system and policy

· Drives a cost-saving culture across all areas of the business

· Develops capability of subordinates towards the achievement of their own performance target

*EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE*

· Minimum of first degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics or any related

· Relevant professional certifications e.g. Chartered Financial Analyst, Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria (ICAN), Association of Certified and Chartered Accountants (ACCA) or its equivalent is required

· MBA or a Masters’ in related discipline is an added advantage

· Minimum of 8 years cognate experience, 3 of which must have been in a Senior Management position of a similar organisation, with evidence of regular engagement with the management team and financial partners

*SKILLS AND COMPETENCIES PROFILE*

*Skills*

1. Financial Strategy

2. Budgeting/Financial Planning

3. Financial Modelling /Analysis

4. Accounting Operations

5. Financial Performance Reporting

6. Tax Planning/ Management

7. Cost Analysis/Management

8. Management Accounting

9. Financial Management

10. Investment Planning /Appraisal

11. Corporate Finance

12. Treasury Management

13. Knowledge of applicable accounting software

*Competencies*

1. Strategic perspective (strategy development and execution)

2. Leadership

3. Problem-solving and analytical thinking

4. Communication (Written, Verbal, Presentation)

5. Attention to detail

6. People Management

7. Relationship Management

8. Risk management

Job Description:

Where you fit in?

As a IT Controls Systems Engineer you will deliver or improve the range of activities necessary to seamlessly integrate and operate one of the world’s largest IT estates. The scope covers service management activities, Hybrid on-premise and Cloud operational landscapes with many integration points; and the integration of the interdependent services of diverse internal and external service providers into end-to-end services which deliver value to Shell.

What is the Role?

This Role will report to the IT Operations Manager working in conjuction with other operations technology security stakeholders to provide services as listed below:

• Ensure the Safe, Secure, and Steady and Reliable production operations on Onshore/Offshore Fields Asset in Nigeria via sustained Process Control Domain (PCD) IT Controls effectiveness and preventing impact to asset integrity.
• Ensure 1st Line Support and request management, SecurePlant and L2/L3 Firewall device management, Central Anti-Virus server & Patch deployment management.
• Co-ordinate Logistics and Scheduling and Maintenance Planning for Control Systems IT Support personnel.
• Supervise and Co-ordinate Core CS-IT activities for SNEPCO PCD activities including: Backup & Disaster recovery, remote access requests and management, end-point protection for hosts, implementation of configuration changes for hosts and network, inventory management
• Carry out deviation management and risk assessment for all facilities within the Nigeria PCD space.
• PCD assurance and assessments – physical security, backup and disaster recovery, remote access, Antivirus and Operating system patches.
• Liaising with Technical Authority for production operations towards ensuring compliance of IT Security within the PCD.
• Drive and Ensure the implementation of Incident Response and Management strategies
• Embed Think Secure practices and raise PCD IT Security awareness of staff working in or interacting with the PCD.

• Ensure the team complies with business controls (e.g. Management of Change, Permit to Work) for applying changes in the PCD IT landscape.
• Ensure that Supportability & Transition is effective for IT- or business-led projects that deliver into the PCD IT landscape.
• Monitor the quality of IT infrastructure services supporting PCD (e.g. WAN, Routers, SecurePlant) and ensure changes to those services do not impact operations without prior notification and approval.
• Advise stakeholders on matters concerning PCD IT Security. Keep proper communication to site staff on imminent threats and on cyber security advisories, highlighting the recommended actions for the teams on-site.
• Manage user access to supported systems (e.g. SecurePlant, Sharepoint Site).
• Coordinate firewall rule reviews with the team members to ensure it is aligned with business requirements and have been risk-assessed appropriately.
• Coordinate, along with the team members, the assurance activities of PCD IT Security controls implemented on-site – supporting both 1st Line of Defence (LoD-1) and 2nd Line of Defence (LoD-2) processes

What we need from you?

• With minimum 3-5 years’ experience in IT business analysis, IT security, IT service delivery and IT infrastructure support. The last three years must be managing operations technology security; Experience in an Oil and Gas industry is preferred.
• Minimum Education or Certification: University degree in Electrical, Electronics, Communications Engineering or Applied Sciences (if further degree in Electronics or Communications Engineering),
• Multiple years of experience in support/administration of IT infrastructure services. Completed Training or Certifications in cybersecurity industry certification such as GICSP or ISC2 CISSP is preferred,
• Certifications in networking, Systems administration and IT service Management such as CCNP (enterprise or security), MCSA, SC100, ITILV4 are other Preferred requirements
• Relevant exposure to Process Automation and Controls Optimization disciplines.
• Good knowledge of Control Systems, IT networks, and IT security
• A strong understanding of IT security standards (DEPs, NIST 800-82, OneITCF)
• Significant understanding of the Upstream business and Joint Venture concept
• Experience with IT and Business Risk Management
• Effective communication and interpersonal skills, suitable for diverse cultures and levels in Production and IT
• Ability to manage relations with peers and senior managers effectively in various organizations

Shell is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

WHY JOIN SHELL?

An innovative place to work

There’s never been a more exciting time to work at Shell. Everyone here is helping solve one of the biggest challenges facing the world today: bringing the benefits of energy to everyone on the planet, whilst managing the risks of climate change.
Join us and you’ll add your talent and imagination to a business with the power to shape the future – whether by investing in renewables, exploring new ways to store energy or developing technology that helps the world to use energy more efficiently.

An inclusive place to work

To power progress together, we need to attract and develop the brightest minds and make sure every voice is heard. Here are just some of the ways we’re nurturing an inclusive environment – one where you can express your ideas, extend your skills and reach your potential.
We’re creating a space where people with disabilities can excel through transparent recruitment process, workplace adjustments and ongoing support in their roles. Feel free to let us know about your circumstances when you apply, and we’ll take it from there.

We’re striving to be a pioneer of an inclusive and diverse workplace, promoting equality for employees regardless of sexual orientation or gender identity.
We consider ourselves a flexible employer and want to support you finding the right balance. We encourage you to discuss this with us in your application.

Combine our creative, collaborative environment and global operations with an impressive range of benefits and joining Shell becomes an inspired career choice.

*Location:* Lagos, Nigeria *(with travel within Nigeria)*

*Industry:* Aviation / Aerospace Supply & Technical Sales

*About Elyon Technik Ltd*

Elyon Technik Ltd is an international equipment and technical supply company serving the aviation, energy, and industrial sectors. With offices in The Woodlands, Texas (USA) and multiple locations across Nigeria, the company works with leading global manufacturers to supply high-quality equipment, parts, and technical solutions.

We are expanding our aviation business in Nigeria and are seeking motivated Technical Sales Associate to develop and manage relationships across the Nigerian aviation ecosystem.

*Position Summary*

The Technical Sales Associate will be responsible for identifying, developing, and closing sales opportunities for aircraft parts, aviation consumables, and technical components within the Nigerian aviation industry.

The successful candidate will work closely with airlines, MRO facilities, ground handling companies, and aviation regulatory stakeholders to supply aircraft components and technical solutions.

*Key Responsibilities*

* Identify and develop sales opportunities for aircraft parts and aviation components within Nigeria.
* Build and maintain relationships with airlines, aviation maintenance organizations (MROs), ground handling companies, and aviation service providers.
* Prepare technical quotations, proposals, and RFQ responses for aviation clients.
* Develop and maintain a pipeline of prospects and convert leads into long-term customers.
* Monitor aviation industry trends, procurement cycles, and upcoming aircraft maintenance schedules.
* Represent the company at aviation conferences, exhibitions, and industry events.
* Coordinate with internal logistics and compliance teams to ensure timely delivery of parts and documentation.
* Maintain accurate CRM records, sales reports, and market intelligence.

*Minimum Qualifications*

* Minimum of 3 years sales experience in a technical role (preferably aviation, engineering, oil & gas, or industrial equipment).
* HND or Bachelor's degree in:
* Sales or Marketing
* Engineering (Mechanical, Aerospace, Electrical, etc.)
* Physical Sciences or related discipline.
* Strong ability to understand technical product specifications and translate them into sales solutions.

*Preferred Qualifications (Highly Desirable)*

* Candidates with the following experience will have a strong advantage:
* Prior experience selling aircraft parts, aviation equipment, or MRO services.
* Familiarity with aircraft part numbers, ATA chapters, and aviation technical documentation.
* Existing relationships within the Nigerian aviation sector such as: Airlines, Maintenance organizations, Aviation service providers, etc.
* Knowledge of aviation procurement systems or parts marketplaces (e.g., PartsBase, ILS, etc.).
* Strong negotiation and contract management skills.

*Required Skills & Attributes*

* Excellent communication and presentation skills.
* Strong technical comprehension and ability to explain complex products to clients.
* Proven business development and closing ability.
* Highly organized with strong attention to detail.
* Self-driven and able to work independently.
* Willingness to travel within Nigeria for client engagement.
* High level of professionalism and integrity.

*What We Offer*

* Competitive salary plus performance-based commission.
* Opportunity to work with global aviation manufacturers and suppliers.
* Career growth within an international technical supply company.
* Exposure to major aviation and infrastructure projects in Nigeria.

*How to Apply*

Interested candidates should send their CV and a short cover letter explaining their aviation or technical sales experience to: sales@elyontek.com and hr@elyontek.com.

Subject Line: Application – Technical Sales Associate (Aviation)

*Note: *Candidates with existing relationships within the Nigerian aviation industry will be given priority consideration.

Job Type: Full-time

Work Location: In person

Company Description

Our Client is a dynamic and innovative oil and gas firm serving the energy needs of the Nigerian market with a renewed focus on downstream excellence, customer-centricity, and operational efficiency. Since its inception, the company has established itself as a leader in the trading, supply, and distribution of petroleum products, leveraging a massive retail network and strategic logistics infrastructure.

As they deepen their commitment to financial sustainability, capital optimization, and fiscal discipline, we seek an outstanding Group Financial Controller to foster financial transparency, control integrity, and compliance with Nigerian and international accounting standards.

Job Description

Group Financial Controller is a critical executive leadership role accountable for enterprise-wide financial assurance, capital management, and risk-based fiscal governance. The incumbent will act as a trusted advisor to the CFO and Senior Executives, fostering financial transparency, control integrity, and compliance with Nigerian and international accounting standards (IFRS). This is a transformational role aimed at driving a forward-looking, agile, and digitally enabled finance function that supports the company’s investment and growth agenda in the energy sector.

Key Responsibilities

• Serve as a key advisor to the CFO and senior leadership on financial strategy, capital allocation, and internal control frameworks.

• Monitor financial trends, emerging economic risks, and tax legislation to ensure the organization is always compliant with FIRS guidelines, oil and gas fiscal regulations, and other applicable financial laws.

• Establish and maintain a comprehensive financial framework, including budgeting policies, treasury monitoring programs, and financial reporting mechanisms.

• Lead the development and enforcement of a strong fiscal discipline and ethics culture across the enterprise to safeguard company assets.

• Collaborate with the Enterprise Risk Management team to ensure robust financial risk assessments are embedded in downstream and midstream trading activities.

• Proactively identify liquidity gaps, emerging financial threats, and areas for cost optimization and operational improvement.

• Develop effective testing, monitoring, and financial control systems to detect and prevent leakages, fraud, or reporting inefficiencies.

Qualifications

• Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related quantitative field. An MBA or a relevant Master’s degree (e.g., MSc in Finance or Financial Management) is highly preferred.

• Professional certifications such as ICAN (Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria) or ACCA are mandatory. Additional certifications like CFA, CITN, or CMA are strongly required.

• Minimum of 12–15 years of progressive experience in financial management, treasury, and corporate accounting, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership capacity—specifically within the oil and gas downstream or midstream sectors.

• Deep knowledge of the Nigerian financial and regulatory environment, including IFRS reporting standards, FIRS tax regulations, Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) foreign exchange guidelines, and the Petroleum Industry Act (PIA).

• Strong experience in capital raising, working capital management, trade finance (Letters of Credit), and managing complex financial audits within a high-volume trading environment.

Leadership Attributes

• Strategic and critical thinker with deep business insight into market volatility, margin optimization, and the financial complexities of the energy industry.

• High ethical standards and a proven track record in organizational trust-building and maintaining rigorous internal controls.

• Exceptional communication, presentation, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to manage relationships with banks, regulators, and the Board.

• Ability to lead change and drive enterprise-wide culture shifts toward fiscal discipline, cost reduction, and financial transparency.

• Digitally aware, with a strong understanding of systems-based controls, ERP systems (such as SAP, Oracle, or Microsoft Dynamics), and advanced financial modeling tools.

company: South Atlantic LNG West Africa Limited (SLNG)
description:
Company: South Atlantic LNG West Africa Limited

Location: Lagos, Nigeria (with project site travel)

Employment Type: Full-time / Contract (project-based)

Role Overview

South Atlantic LNG West Africa Limited is seeking an experienced Project Manager (Owner’s Representative) to take full ownership of the delivery of its LNG, gas processing, and virtual pipeline infrastructure projects in Nigeria.

Working alongside an appointed Owner’s Engineer, the Project Manager will be responsible for overall project outcomes — schedule, cost, contracts, risk, and stakeholder coordination — acting as the single point of accountability on behalf of the company.

Key Responsibilities

Project Delivery & Governance

* Act as the Owner’s single point of responsibility for project execution from contract award through commissioning and handover.
* Develop and maintain the integrated project execution plan, master schedule, and cost control framework.
* Ensure alignment between project objectives, commercial strategy, and execution decisions.

Contractor & Consultant Management

* Manage EPC contractors, Owner’s Engineer, and other consultants, ensuring clear scope, accountability, and performance.
* Chair project progress meetings and drive resolution of issues, delays, and interface risks.
* Ensure Owner’s Engineer findings are translated into timely commercial and execution decisions.

Commercial & Contract Management

* Administer EPC and major supply contracts on behalf of the Owner.
* Review and recommend approval or rejection of variations, claims, extensions of time, and milestone payments.
* Monitor contractor cash flow requests against actual progress and contract terms.

Schedule, Cost & Risk Control

* Maintain control of project budget and forecast final project cost.
* Identify schedule and cost risks early and implement mitigation measures.
* Ensure effective change management and documentation of decisions.

Regulatory, Permitting & Stakeholder Coordination

* Coordinate permitting and regulatory approvals with NUPRC, NESREA, state authorities, and other agencies.
* Manage interfaces with landowners, host communities, utilities, logistics providers, and government stakeholders.
* Support local content compliance and community engagement activities.

Commissioning & Handover

* Coordinate readiness for commissioning, testing, and handover in collaboration with EPC and Owner’s Engineer.
* Ensure completion of punch lists, documentation, and operational handover requirements.

Reporting & Management Support

* Provide concise, decision-focused reports to company leadership and investors.
* Escalate critical issues promptly with clear recommendations.

Qualifications & Experience

Education

* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related discipline.

Experience

* 10–15 years experience in project delivery roles.
* Proven track record managing owner-side delivery of oil & gas, LNG, gas processing, power, or industrial infrastructure projects.
* Experience working with EPC contracts and Owner’s Engineers.
* Strong exposure to Nigerian regulatory and operating environments.

Professional Credentials

* PMP, PRINCE2, or equivalent certification is an advantage.
* Engineering or project management professional registration is desirable.

Skills & Competencies

* Strong commercial and contractual awareness.
* Excellent leadership, coordination, and decision-making skills.
* Ability to manage multiple stakeholders and competing priorities.
* Clear communicator with strong reporting discipline.
* Comfortable operating in a lean, fast-moving organization.

Why Join South Atlantic LNG West Africa Limited?

* Lead the delivery of strategic LNG and virtual pipeline projects critical to Nigeria’s energy transition.
* High-impact role with direct access to company leadership and decision-making authority.
* Opportunity to shape execution culture and grow into a senior delivery or executive role as the company scales.

Remuneration - N350, 000 to N400, 000 monthly gross

About the Role

South Atlantic LNG West Africa Limited (SLNG) is an indigenous Nigerian gas company developing a 20MMscf LNG facility, commencing with Phase 1 (5MMscf). We are seeking a highly competent Administration & Corporate Services Manager to manage office operations and temporarily oversee HR and Finance functions within a lean, multi-disciplinary team.

* Office & Administration: Manage day-to-day office operations, logistics, vendor coordination, site support, and general administration.
* Human Resources (Interim): Handle recruitment support, onboarding, personnel records, payroll coordination, leave management, and local content reporting.

* Ensure compliance with Nigerian labour, tax, and regulatory requirements.
* Support management reporting and preparation of operational updates.
* Assist with the transition to dedicated HR and Finance functions in Phase 2.

* Strong organisational, planning, and problem-solving skills.

description:
Company: South Atlantic LNG West Africa Limited

Location: Lagos, Nigeria

Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview

South Atlantic LNG West Africa Limited is seeking a hands-on Accountant / Finance Manager to build and manage the company’s finance function from the ground up. This role is critical to the success of our LNG, gas processing, and virtual pipeline projects and requires someone comfortable operating in a lean, fast-moving start-up environment with minimal bureaucracy.

The successful candidate will take ownership of day-to-day accounting, cash management, statutory compliance, and financial support for project development and investment decisions.

Key Responsibilities

End-to-End Accounting & Finance Operations

* Set up, maintain, and manage the company’s accounting systems, records, and processes.
* Prepare monthly management accounts, financial reports, and cash flow statements.
* Handle bookkeeping, reconciliations, fixed assets, accruals, and expenses.

Cash Flow & Treasury Management

* Manage bank accounts, payment processing, and vendor settlements.
* Track project-related cash flows and funding requirements.
* Ensure strict cash discipline in line with start-up priorities.

Budgeting, Cost Control & Project Finance Support

* Prepare budgets and forecasts for corporate and project activities.
* Track project costs for LNG facilities, truck loading operations, and virtual pipeline logistics.
* Support financial models, pricing structures, and commercial proposals.

Tax, Statutory & Regulatory Compliance

* Prepare and file statutory returns including VAT, WHT, PAYE, and CIT.
* Liaise with auditors, tax consultants, banks, and regulators (FIRS, State IRS, CAC).
* Support annual audits and ensure compliance with Nigerian financial regulations.

Governance, Controls & Process Development

* Establish practical financial controls suitable for a lean organization.
* Draft and implement basic finance policies and approval processes.
* Identify financial and operational risks and proactively manage them.

Founder & Management Support

* Work closely with company leadership to support investment decisions, funding discussions, and board reporting.
* Provide financial insights to guide project prioritization and growth strategy.

Qualifications & Experience

Education

* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.

Professional Qualification

* ACA, ACCA, or equivalent (strongly preferred).

Experience

* 5–10 years relevant experience, ideally in a start-up, SME, or project-based business.
* Experience in energy, oil & gas, infrastructure, logistics, or industrial projects is an advantage.
* Proven ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
